subject: Zip archive extraction without path traversal validation
truth: Stop combining archive entry filenames with a destination directory without resolving and validating the full path; use Path.GetFullPath on both the combined path and the destination directory, then verify the resolved output path starts with the resolved destination directory.
quote: output paths constructed from zip archive entries are validated to prevent writing files to unexpected locations
code_bad:
string destFileName = Path.Combine(destDirectory, entry.FullName);
        entry.ExtractToFile(destFileName);

subject: Android DoS via uncaught NumberFormatException
truth: Always use Intent.getIntExtra(name, defaultValue), getFloatExtra(name, defaultValue), or getDoubleExtra(name, defaultValue) instead of parsing string extras, because Android components cannot throw checked exceptions and an invalid extra crashes the app.
quote: If the application allows external inputs, an attacker can send an invalid number as intent extra to trigger NFE, which introduces local Denial of Service (Dos) attack.
code_bad:
String minPriceStr = getIntent().getStringExtra("priceMin");
double minPrice = Double.parseDouble(minPriceStr);
code_good:
int width = getIntent().getIntExtra("width", 0);
int height = getIntent().getIntExtra("height", 0);

subject: Java temp file/dir creation without explicit POSIX permissions
truth: When creating files or directories in the system temp dir with a non-random name, explicitly set POSIX permissions to owner-only using PosixFilePermissions.asFileAttribute(EnumSet.of(PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ, PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE)).
quote: File has permissions `-rw-r--r--`
code_bad: Files.createFile(tempDirChildFile.toPath()); // BAD: File has permissions `-rw-r--r--`
code_good:
Files.createFile(
    tempChildFile.toPath(),
    PosixFilePermissions.asFileAttribute(EnumSet.of(PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ, PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE))
); // GOOD: Good has permissions `-rw-------`

subject: XSS sink identification in external APIs
truth: Stop assuming all external API results are irrelevant; when an external API like HttpResponse.Write receives untrusted data, evaluate whether it is a security-relevant sink (e.g., XSS) and confirm it is handled by the appropriate security query.
quote: If the query were to return the API System.Web.HttpResponse.Write(string) [param 0] then we should first consider whether this a security relevant sink

subject: TokenValidationParameters delegate validators
truth: Stop setting TokenValidationParameter validation delegates (AudienceValidator, IssuerValidator, LifetimeValidator) to always return true; implement proper validation logic that can return false or throw the appropriate SecurityToken*Exception.
quote: By setting critical TokenValidationParameter validation delegates to always return true, important authentication safeguards are disabled.
code_bad: parameters.AudienceValidator = (audiences, token, tvp) => { return true; };

subject: BeanShell injection via StaticScriptSource.setScript()
truth: Stop setting untrusted user input via StaticScriptSource.setScript() and then evaluating it; instead, avoid using untrusted input in BeanShell expressions or run them in a sandbox that allows accessing only explicitly allowed classes.
quote: If a BeanShell expression is built using attacker-controlled data, and then evaluated, then it may allow the attacker to run arbitrary code.

subject: ZipSlip symlink extraction validation
truth: Resolve pre-existing symbolic links with filepath.EvalSymlinks before checking that a link's target is within the destination directory; purely syntactic checks like filepath.Rel are ineffective against symlink-based path traversal.
quote: purely syntactic means are used to check

subject: IPv6-transition SSRF bypass
truth: Normalize the host before validating it: parse the address with a transition-aware library and unwrap IPv4-mapped, NAT64 and 6to4 forms to their embedded IPv4 address, then apply the private-range check to the normalized value.
quote: When such a guard inspects only the dotted-quad IPv4 form and never unwraps IPv6-transition representations, it can be bypassed

subject: Unanchored regex alternatives
truth: When writing regex with multiple host alternatives, anchor each alternative individually; /^www\.example\.com|beta\.example\.com/ is parsed as /(^www\.example\.com)|(beta\.example\.com)/ and matches evil.beta.example.com.
quote: the regular expression /^www\.example\.com|beta\.example\.com/ will match the host evil.beta.example.com, because the regular expression is parsed as /(^www\.example\.com)|(beta\.example\.com)/

subject: LDAP search filter construction with string concatenation
truth: Stop building LDAP search filters by concatenating user input into strings; use Filter.createEqualityFilter (UnboundID), equal() (Apache), or LdapQueryBuilder.where().is() (Spring) which handle escaping automatically.
quote: If possible build the LDAP query using framework helper methods, for example from Spring's LdapQueryBuilder and LdapNameBuilder, instead of string concatenation.
code_bad: String filter = "username=" + userName;
code_good: Filter safeFilter = Filter.createEqualityFilter("username", username);

subject: LDAP query construction with string concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into LDAP DNs and search filters; use framework helpers like Spring's LdapQueryBuilder/LdapNameBuilder, UnboundID Filter/DN, or Apache LDAP API's Dn/Rdn/FilterBuilder instead.
quote: If an LDAP query is built using string concatenation, and the components of the concatenation include user input, a user is likely to be able to run malicious LDAP queries.

subject: Python regex engine catastrophic backtracking
truth: Stop assuming Python's re module is safe for user-supplied regex patterns; the backtracking NFA engine has worst-case exponential time complexity, so validate input length or use the regex module with time limits.
quote: The worst-case time complexity of such an automaton can be polynomial or even exponential

subject: new vs new(std::nothrow) error handling mismatch
truth: Stop checking for null after 'new T' (which throws on failure); instead use try/catch for std::bad_alloc. Stop catching exceptions after 'new(std::nothrow) T' (which returns null); instead check for null pointer.
quote: the allocation will throw an unhandled exception

subject: ZipSlip symlink extraction
truth: When extracting symbolic links from archives, resolve previously-extracted symlinks using filepath.EvalSymlinks before validating the destination path; a pair of crafted symlinks can bypass syntactic path checks.
quote: a pair of links like subdir/parent -> .. followed by escape -> subdir/parent/.. -> subdir/../.. leaves a link pointing to the parent of the archive root

subject: Log4j JNDI injection via user-controlled log messages
truth: Do not treat setting log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true or upgrading to Log4j 2.15.0 alone as sufficient remediation for Log4Shell — the 2.15.0 fix was bypassed within days (CVE-2021-45046, RCE in non-default configs using Pattern Layout with Context Lookup/Thread Context Map). Upgrade to Log4j 2.17.1 or later (2.12.4 for Java 7, 2.3.2 for Java 6) — the first release addressing the full chain of CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-45105 (DoS via uncontrolled recursion, fixed 2.17.0), and CVE-2021-44832 (JDBC Appender RCE, fixed 2.17.1). If upgrading is not immediately possible, remove the JndiLookup class from the classpath as an interim mitigation, but plan to upgrade to 2.17.1+ (or the current maintained 2.x/3.x release) as the durable fix.
quote: Log4j will make a JNDI lookup on the specified LDAP server and potentially load arbitrary code
_audit_fix: STALE: 2.15.0 was found bypassable within days (CVE-2021-45046, then the DoS CVE-2021-45105 and CVE-2021-44832), and Apache/CISA now state that log4j2.formatMsgNoLooku

subject: Spring Framework unsafe service exporters (CVE-2016-1000027)
truth: Do not assume Spring's HttpInvokerServiceExporter or RmiServiceExporter are safe because they are part of the framework; they use ObjectInputStream or Hessian deserialization which allows remote code execution.
quote: It is regarded as a design limitation, and can be mitigated but not fixed outright.

subject: LDAP injection via string formatting in ldap3.Connection.search
truth: Stop building LDAP DNs and search filters with unsanitized user input via string formatting; escape DN components with ldap3.utils.dn.escape_rdn and filter components with ldap3.utils.conv.escape_filter_chars.
quote: In Python2, user input should be escaped with ldap.dn.escape_dn_chars or ldap.filter.escape_filter_chars, while in Python3, user input should be escaped with ldap3.utils.dn.escape_rdn or ldap3.utils.c
code_bad:
from flask import request, Flask
import ldap3


@app.route("/normal")
def normal():
    unsafe_dc = request.args['dc']
    unsafe_filter = request.args['username']

    dn = "dc={}".format(unsafe_dc)
    search_filter = "(user={})".format(unsafe_filter)

    srv = ldap3.Server('ldap://127.0.0.1')
    conn = ldap3.Connection(srv, user=dn, auto_bind=True)
    conn.search(dn, search_filter)
code_good:
from flask import request, Flask
import ldap3
from ldap3.utils.dn import escape_rdn
from ldap3.utils.conv import escape_filter_chars


@app.route("/normal")
def normal():
    unsafe_dc = request.args['dc']
    unsafe_filter = request.args['username']

    safe_dc = escape_rdn(unsafe_dc)
    safe_filter = escape_filter_chars(unsafe_filter)

    dn = "dc={}".format(safe_dc)
    search_filter = "(user={})".format(safe_filter)

    srv = ldap3.Server('ldap://127.0.0.1')
    conn = ldap3.Connection(srv, user=dn, auto_bind=True)
    conn.search(dn, search_filter)

subject: Custom TrustManager with empty checkServerTrusted
truth: Stop using a custom TrustManager whose checkServerTrusted method never throws a CertificateException; instead load the specific self-signed certificate into a KeyStore and use the default TrustManagerFactory.
quote: If the checkServerTrusted method of a TrustManager never throws a CertificateException, it trusts every certificate.

subject: Android WebView with JavaScript and cross-origin access enabled loading external URLs
truth: Stop enabling both setJavaScriptEnabled(true) and setAllowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs(true) when loading externally controlled URLs in WebView; disable cross-origin file access or only load trusted content.
quote: Have both JavaScript and cross-origin resource access enabled in webview while

subject: Missing path validation rules for user-controlled filenames
truth: Validate user input before using it to construct a file path: do not allow more than a single '.' character, do not allow directory separators such as '/' or '\', and use a whitelist of known good patterns.
quote: Validate user input before using it to construct a file path, either using an off-the-shelf library like ActiveStorage::Filename#sanitized in Rails, or by performing custom validation.

subject: Token validation parameters
truth: Stop setting TokenValidationParameters properties (RequireExpirationTime, ValidateAudience, ValidateIssuer, ValidateLifetime) to false; set them to true or remove the assignment since the default is true.
quote: Turning off validation can lead to security holes by allowing untrusted tokens to make it through validation.

subject: strconv.Quote limitations for SQL escaping
truth: Do not use strconv.Quote for SQL escaping: it only supports double quotes (not single quotes) and wraps the entire string in quotes, so it cannot be used for substrings within a larger SQL string literal.
quote: It only supports double quotes, not single quotes

subject: format string with user input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly as the format string to String.format, System.out.format, or Formatter.format; instead pass '%s' as the format string and the user input as a trailing argument.
quote: user provided value is included in the format string

subject: Unvalidated file path after root prefix check
truth: Always normalize the file path with path.resolve and fs.realpathSync before checking it starts with the root folder, because an unnormalized path starting with the root can still escape via '..' segments.
quote: You should always normalize the file path since an unnormalized path that starts with the root folder can still be used to access files outside the root folder.
code_bad:
// BAD: This function uses unsanitized input that can read any file on the file system.
res.write(fs.readFileSync(ROOT + filePath, 'utf8'));

subject: Decompression bomb (ZIP)
truth: When decompressing a user-provided compressed file, verify the decompression ratio or decompress the files within a loop byte by byte to be able to manage the decompressed size in each cycle of the loop.
quote: the decompressed file size is not checked before decompression, exposing the application to a denial of service

subject: Java String.matches() and Pattern.matches() dot newline bypass
truth: Stop using String.matches(regex) or Pattern.matches(regex, string) for path validation; these methods do not use DOTALL mode and a dot will not match \n or \r, allowing bypass with line break characters.
quote: A string with line return e.g. `/protected/%0dxyz` can bypass the path check

subject: URL redirect with user input
truth: Stop putting user input directly into a redirect URL without validation; instead, validate against a whitelist of authorized redirects or check that the target is a relative URL or on a known good host.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.
code_bad: response.sendRedirect(request.getParameter("target"));

subject: File path construction from user input
truth: Validate user input before using it to construct a file path; check that the normalized path is relative and does not contain '..' components, or check that the path is contained within a safe folder.
quote: Accessing paths controlled by users can allow an attacker to access unexpected resources
code_bad: BufferedReader fileReader = new BufferedReader(new FileReader(filename));

subject: Unmodeled taint propagation through external APIs
truth: Stop treating external API calls as opaque; if an external API propagates taint from its arguments to its return value or receiver, add explicit taint-step modeling so the analysis can track the flow.
quote: If the result represents a call to an external API which transfers taint, add the appropriate modeling, and re-run the query to determine what new results have appeared due to this additional modeling

subject: Android NumberFormatException from intent extras
truth: Stop parsing intent extras as strings with Double.parseDouble() or similar; use Android's built-in getIntExtra(), getFloatExtra(), getDoubleExtra() etc. which handle NumberFormatException internally.
quote: NumberFormatException (NFE) thrown but not caught by an Android application will crash the application
code_bad:
String minPriceStr = getIntent().getStringExtra("priceMin");
double minPrice = Double.parseDouble(minPriceStr);
code_good:
int width = getIntent().getIntExtra("width", 0);
int height = getIntent().getIntExtra("height", 0);

subject: SSRF bypassing firewalls via internal host
truth: Stop assuming user-controlled URL hosts only reach external servers; an attacker can target internal.example.com by crafting input like 'internal.example.com/#' to bypass the intended domain suffix.
quote: attackers can choose the value of target to be anything they want. For instance, the attacker can choose "internal.example.com/#" as the target, causing the URL used in the request to be "https://internal.example.com/#.example.com/data"

subject: Block-list classloader bypass via compile-time metaprogramming
truth: Stop only blocking runtime classes like Runtime and ProcessBuilder; also block groovy.transform.ASTTest, groovy.lang.GrabConfig, and org.buildobjects.process.ProcBuilder to prevent compile-time RCE.
quote: block `groovy.transform.ASTTest`, `groovy.lang.GrabConfig` and `org.buildobjects.process.ProcBuilder` to prevent compile-time RCE
code_bad: /* override `loadClass` here to prevent loading sensitive classes, such as `java.lang.Runtime`, `java.lang.ProcessBuilder`, `java.lang.System`, etc.  */
code_good: /* Note we must also block `groovy.transform.ASTTest`, `groovy.lang.GrabConfig` and `org.buildobjects.process.ProcBuilder` to prevent compile-time RCE. */

subject: Array bypass of string sanitizers
truth: Stop assuming HTTP request parameters are always strings; when multiple parameters with the same name are sent, the value becomes an array, and array.indexOf behaves differently from string.indexOf.
quote: if an attacker sends multiple request parameters with the same name, then the request parameter is represented as an array instead

subject: filepath.Join with user input
truth: Stop assuming filepath.Join sanitizes user input; a malicious user could enter a file name containing special characters like '../../etc/passwd' which still resolves outside the intended directory.
quote: it appears that the user is restricted to opening a file within the "user" home directory. However, a malicious user could enter a file name containing special characters
code_bad:
data, _ = ioutil.ReadFile(filepath.Join("/home/user/", path))
w.Write(data)

subject: ReDoS-vulnerable regex with ambiguous repetition
truth: Stop using ambiguous repetitions like r* or r+ where the sub-expression r can match the same string in multiple ways; instead rewrite the regex to remove ambiguity or use a non-backtracking engine.
quote: a repetition of the form r* or r+ where the sub-expression r is ambiguous in the sense that it can match some string in multiple ways

subject: External API as implicit taint step
truth: Stop assuming an external API only consumes data; treat every external API that returns a value derived from its arguments as a potential taint propagation step until explicitly modeled otherwise.
quote: this should be reviewed as a possible taint step, because tainted data would flow from the 0th argument to the result of the call.

subject: Unmodeled external API taint sinks
truth: Stop treating unmodeled external APIs as safe; if an external API receives untrusted data and is not modeled as a taint step or sink, it may silently propagate taint or introduce vulnerabilities.
quote: External APIs may be from the .NET standard library, third-party dependencies or from internal dependencies.

subject: format specifier positional access
truth: Stop using user-controlled format strings that can access arguments by position; a malicious %1$tm or %1$te specifier can leak month/day from a Calendar argument when only the year was intended.
quote: a user-specified format string may include a specifier to access the month and day

subject: Android KeyStore user authentication validity duration
truth: Stop using setUserAuthenticationValidityDurationSeconds with a positive value; set it to -1 or use setUserAuthenticationParameters(0, AUTH_BIOMETRIC_STRONG) to require biometric auth every time.
quote: setUserAuthenticationValidityDurationSeconds, if used, should be set to -1; otherwise, non-biometric (less secure) credentials can be used to access the key.

subject: Spring Framework unsafe service exporters (HttpInvokerServiceExporter, SimpleHttpInvokerServiceExporter, RmiServiceExporter, HessianServiceExporter)
truth: Stop using HttpInvokerServiceExporter, SimpleHttpInvokerServiceExporter, RmiServiceExporter, or HessianServiceExporter; use JSON-based endpoints with properly configured deserialization instead.
quote: Avoid using unsafe service exporters. Instead, use other message formats for API endpoints (for example, JSON)

subject: Missing path traversal validation after path resolution
truth: After resolving the combined path with Path.GetFullPath, always verify it starts with the resolved destination directory (appended with Path.DirectorySeparatorChar) to prevent zip-slip attacks.
quote: Validate that the resolved output path StartsWith the resolved destination directory, aborting if this is not true
code_bad:
string destFileName = Path.Combine(destDirectory, entry.FullName);
        entry.ExtractToFile(destFileName);

subject: Velocity template injection from HTTP parameter
truth: Stop reading user input from request.getParameter() and passing it directly as a template string to Velocity.evaluate(); use a constant template and pass user data as context variables instead.
quote: untrusted HTTP parameter code is used as a Velocity template string

subject: Password hashing without salt using SHA256 in C#
truth: Stop hashing passwords with SHA256 without a salt; generate a cryptographically random salt, combine it with the password bytes, hash the combined bytes, and store the salt alongside the hash.
quote: Without a salt, it is much easier for attackers to pre-compute the hash value using dictionary attack techniques such as rainbow tables to crack passwords.

subject: Ruby format string denial of service via exception
truth: A malicious user providing format specifiers like %s as their username causes Kernel.printf to throw an exception due to too few arguments, leading to denial of service or information leakage.
quote: if a malicious user provides a format specified such as %s as their user name, Kernel.printf will throw an exception as there are too few arguments to satisfy the format
code_bad: printf("Unauthorised access attempt by #{params[:user]}: %s", request.ip)
code_good: printf("Unauthorised access attempt by %s: %s", params[:user], request.ip)

subject: XPathExpression.Compile with concatenated strings
truth: Pre-compile the XPath query with variable placeholders ($varname) and use a custom XsltContext to resolve variables at runtime, instead of concatenating user input into the expression string.
quote: pre-compile the query and use variable references to include the user input

subject: FileManager path access from user input
truth: Stop using user-controlled strings directly with FileManager file access methods; validate and normalize the path using FilePath.lexicallyNormalized() and check against a known safe prefix.
quote: Accessing paths controlled by users can expose resources to attackers
code_bad: return fm.contents(atPath: path)

subject: narrow integer overflow in loop
truth: Do not use a narrow type (short, int) as a loop counter when comparing against a wider type (long); the narrow type will overflow before reaching the wider value, causing an infinite loop.
quote: the narrower value may overflow. This can lead to an infinite loop.

subject: Token validation failure handling
truth: Stop returning true for all paths in token validation delegates; throw SecurityTokenInvalidAudienceException or SecurityTokenInvalidLifetimeException to fail validation when appropriate.
quote: throw SecurityTokenInvalidAudienceException or SecurityTokenInvalidLifetimeException in failure cases when you want to fail validation and have other cases pass by returning true

subject: Allowlist validation before outbound HTTP requests
truth: Do not validate user-supplied URLs by checking against a blocklist or prefix; instead maintain an allowlist of authorized URLs on the server and choose from that list based on the input.
quote: maintain a list of authorized URLs on the server; then choose from that list based on the input provided

subject: Android ContentProvider permissions
truth: Always specify both android:readPermission and android:writePermission (or use android:permission) on exported ContentProviders; setting only one leaves the other operation unprotected.
quote: If you do not specify the permission required to perform an operation, the application will implicitly have access to perform that operation.
code_bad:
<provider
    android:name=".MyContentProvider"
    android:authorities="table"
    android:enabled="true"
    android:exported="true"
    android:readPermission="android.permission.MANAGE_DOCUMENTS">
</provider>
code_good:
<provider
    android:name=".MyContentProvider"
    android:authorities="table"
    android:enabled="true"
    android:exported="true"
    android:writePermission="android.permission.MANAGE_DOCUMENTS"
    android:readPermission="android.permission.MANAGE_DOCUMENTS">
</provider>

subject: LDAPS endpoint identification disabled via system property
truth: Stop disabling LDAPS endpoint identification via System.setProperty("com.sun.jndi.ldap.object.disableEndpointIdentification", "true"); instead, leave the default enabled check in place.
quote: frequently developers simply disable the LDAPS endpoint check

subject: Spring HttpInvokerServiceExporter
truth: Stop using HttpInvokerServiceExporter, SimpleHttpInvokerServiceExporter, RmiServiceExporter, or HessianServiceExporter; they deserialize untrusted data and enable remote code execution.
quote: If a remote attacker can reach endpoints created by the exporters, it results in remote code execution in the worst case.

subject: Dynamic method access on global object
truth: Stop invoking user-controlled method names on the global object (window[name]); instead, store API methods in a dedicated object or Map and validate the method name against a whitelist.
quote: Calling a user-controlled method on certain objects can lead to invocation of unsafe functions, such as eval or the Function constructor.

subject: URL allowlist bypass via substring
truth: Stop using 'in' to check if a host appears anywhere in a URL string; an attacker can embed the allowed host anywhere (e.g., 'evil-example.net/example.com') to bypass the check silently.
quote: the attacker can simply add example.com anywhere in the url

subject: ProcessName-to-hash flow as backdoor indicator
truth: Stop flowing ProcessName into a hash function; this pattern matches Solorigate-style backdoors that compare hashed process names against an obfuscated blocklist of antivirus processes.
quote: code flow from ProcessName property on the Process class into a hash function

subject: void return type in Spring MVC controller
truth: Stop using void return type in Spring MVC controllers when the URI path contains user input; add an HttpServletResponse parameter to prevent implicit view name resolution from the URI.
quote: returns void, so view name is taken from URI

subject: Custom revocation checker must be registered when disabling default
truth: When disabling default revocation checking with setRevocationEnabled(false), always register a custom PKIXRevocationChecker via addCertPathChecker() to maintain revocation validation.
quote: If an application doesn't set a custom PKIXRevocationChecker via PKIXParameters.addCertPathChecker() or PKIXParameters.setCertPathCheckers() methods, then revocation checking is not going to happen.

subject: Decompression bomb (gzip/any compression)
truth: Attackers can create a huge file by just repeating a single byte and compress it to a small file — always enforce a decompressed size threshold when extracting user-provided archives.
quote: Attackers can create a huge file by just repeating a single byte and compress it to a small file

subject: Express.js POST handler authentication assumption
truth: Do not assume cookie-based session authentication alone protects POST endpoints from CSRF; an attacker can forge requests on behalf of an authenticated user visiting a malicious site.
quote: An attacker can submit a POST changeEmail request on behalf of a user who visited a malicious website

subject: urlparse misparsing mistyped URLs
truth: Mistyped URLs like https:/example.com or https:///example.com have empty netloc in urlparse but browsers still redirect to the external host; strip backslashes and validate carefully.
quote: Mistyped URLs such as https:/example.com or https:///example.com are parsed as having an empty netloc attribute, while browsers will still redirect to the correct site.

subject: narrow integer overflow in loop condition
truth: Stop using a narrow integer type as a loop counter when the termination bound exceeds the narrow type's maximum; the counter will overflow silently and the loop will never terminate.
quote: the narrower value may overflow. This can lead to an infinite loop

subject: Backslash-escaping single quotes in Go strings
truth: Replace the fact. Do not teach manual backslash/quote string-escaping as a generic SQL fix for CWE-089: it is DB-dialect dependent and, for PostgreSQL's default (standard_conforming_strings = on since 9.1), it is actively incorrect — backslash is not an escape character there, single quotes must be doubled ('') instead, and applying the backslash-then-quote scheme neither protects the data nor prevents the injection. Even in MySQL, where backslash escaping is the default behavior, this approach has documented bypasses (e.g. charset/encoding-based string-break attacks defeating mysql_real_escape_string). Corrected truth: "Never build SQL via manual string-escaping and concatenation — use parameterized queries / prepared statements (e.g. Go's database/sql placeholder args: db.Query(\"... WHERE x = ?\", val) or $1-style for pq/pgx) so the driver handles quoting correctly for the target dialect. Manual quote-escaping (including 'escape backslash before quote') is a fragile, dialect-specific anti-pattern flagged by tools like CodeQL's go/string-break query, not a recommended mitigation for CWE-089."
quote: any existing backslash characters in the string must be escaped first, so that they do not interfere with the escaping of single quotes
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The escaping-order detail is technically accurate but the fact teaches manual string-escaping as the fix for CWE-089 SQL injection, which is itself the discoura

subject: LDAP injection via unencoded user input in DN and filter
truth: Always encode user input for both DN (encodeForDN) and search filter (encodeForLDAP) using OWASP ESAPI, or use framework builders that auto-escape, to prevent LDAP injection attacks.
quote: If user input must be included in an LDAP query, it should be escaped to avoid a malicious user providing special characters that change the meaning of the query.

subject: Android WebView with JavaScript enabled and setAllowFileAccessFromFileURLs(true) loading untrusted URLs
truth: Stop calling setAllowFileAccessFromFileURLs(true) when JavaScript is enabled and WebView loads untrusted content; this allows file:// context scripts to access arbitrary local files.
quote: Enabling these settings allows malicious scripts loaded in a file:// context to launch cross-site scripting attacks, accessing arbitrary local files

subject: json.loads on user input before MongoDB query
truth: Stop using json.loads() on user-controlled strings and passing the result directly into MongoDB find()/find_one(); the parsed object can inject query operators like $ne, $regex, $gt.
quote: In order for the user-controlled source to taint the NoSQL query, the user-controller source must be converted into a Python object using something like json.loads or xmltodict.parse.
code_bad:
unsanitized_search = request.args['search']
json_search = json.loads(unsanitized_search)
result = mongo.db.user.find({'name': json_search})
code_good:
unsafe_search = request.args['search']
json_search = json.loads(unsafe_search)
safe_search = sanitize(unsanitized_search)
result = client.db.collection.find_one({'data': safe_search})

associative: user input to dict for MongoDB

subject: Removing '../' sequences from user input
truth: Stop removing '../' sequences as a sanitization strategy; the input could still contain a path separator followed by '..' such as '.../...//' which collapses to '../' after removal.
quote: removing "../" sequences is not sufficient, since the input could still contain a path separator followed by "..". For example, the input "

subject: Password leakage via URL logging
truth: Stop putting passwords in GET query strings because sensitive information within URLs may be logged in various locations including the user's browser, web server, and proxy servers.
quote: Sensitive information within URLs may be logged in various locations, including the user's browser, the web server, and any forward or reverse proxy servers

subject: External API calls with untrusted data
truth: Stop passing unsanitized untrusted data directly to external API calls; validate and sanitize all untrusted input before passing it to any function not defined in your source code.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues

subject: Ignoring external API sink results
truth: Stop dismissing external API results as uninteresting; first verify whether the highlighted API is a known security sink (XSS, injection, etc.) before classifying it as irrelevant.
quote: If the result highlights a known sink, no action is required. If the result highlights an unknown sink for a problem, then add modeling for the sink to the relevant query.

subject: Assembly.LoadFile unrestricted path
truth: Assembly.LoadFile with an unrestricted user-supplied path lets an attacker load any DLL on the system, including attacker-uploaded assemblies, leading to arbitrary code execution.
quote: allows the user to load any assembly installed on the system

subject: JxBrowser certificate validation
truth: Stop using JxBrowser <6.24 without a custom LoadHandler that cancels on certificate errors; add a LoadHandler whose onCertificateError returns true to reject invalid certificates.
quote: by default ignore any HTTPS certificate errors thereby allowing man-in-the-middle attacks

subject: Implicit wrapping Intent for PendingIntent
truth: When adding a PendingIntent as an extra of another Intent, that wrapping Intent must also have an explicit destination component so it is not delivered to untrusted applications.
quote: When you add the PendingIntent as an extra of another Intent, make sure that this second Intent also has an explicit destination component, so that it is not delivered to untrusted applications.

subject: IDOR in ASP.NET Core MVC Controller Actions
truth: Always call an authorization service (e.g., AuthorizeAsync) or use the [Authorize] attribute to verify the user owns or is permitted to access the resource identified by the ID.
quote: Ensure that the current user is authorized to access the resource of the provided ID.

subject: dangerous native function calls (P/Invoke)
truth: Stop calling dangerous native functions like NtCreateProcess, NtCreateThreadEx, or NtWriteVirtualMemory via P/Invoke; they are commonly used for backdoors and process injection.
quote: finds native calls to external functions that are often used in creating backdoors or are generally attributed to unsafe code practices

subject: Android biometric authentication callback pattern
truth: Do not call grantAccess() directly inside onAuthenticationSucceeded; instead use the CryptoObject from the result to decrypt a secret that proves genuine biometric verification.
quote: if this authentication does not use a KeyStore-backed key, it can be bypassed by a privileged malicious application, or by an attacker with physical access using application hooking tools such as Frida

subject: CORS with credentials
truth: Stop setting Access-Control-Allow-Origin to user-controlled Origin header values when Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is true; validate the origin against an allowlist instead.
quote: // BAD -> User controlled CORS header being set here.
code_bad:
response.addHeader("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", url); // BAD -> User controlled CORS header being set here.
response.addHeader("Access-Control-Allow-Credentials", "true");

subject: JMX-RMI server initialization with null/empty environment
truth: Always pass an environment with a deserialization filter to JMXConnectorServerFactory.newJMXConnectorServer() or RMIConnectorServer constructor; never pass null or an empty map.
quote: the JMX-RMI service is vulnerable in the default configuration. For this reason an initialization with a null environment is also vulnerable.

subject: User prompt injection via string concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating untrusted user input directly into user-role prompt strings; validate against an allowlist and use input guardrails before including any user-controlled data.
quote: user-controlled data is inserted directly into a user-role prompt without any validation, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary instructions

subject: Type confusion through parameter tampering
truth: Check that the request parameter is a string (typeof file !== 'string') before calling string methods like indexOf, since multiple parameters with the same name become an array.
quote: However, sanitizing request parameters assuming they have type String and using the builtin string methods such as String.prototype.indexOf is susceptible to type confusion attacks.

subject: SSRF via internal network boundary bypass
truth: Do not allow user-controlled hostnames in outgoing requests from a server connected to an internal network, as this can bypass the network boundary and target internal services.
quote: If the server performing the request is connected to an internal network, this can give an attacker the means to bypass the network boundary and make requests against internal services.

subject: missing bounds check before integer downcast
truth: If you must parse with a larger bit size and then downcast, add explicit upper and lower bounds checks against math.MaxInt32 or the appropriate math constant before converting.
quote: add upper (and lower) bound checks specific to each type and bit size (you can find the minimum and maximum value for each type in the math package).

subject: format string exception from user input
truth: Stop letting user-controlled format strings cause exceptions; extra format specifiers from untrusted input will throw an exception if specifier type doesn't match the argument.
quote: extra format specifiers that cause an exception to be thrown

subject: CORS with credentials and dynamic origin
truth: When Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is true, never set Access-Control-Allow-Origin from user-controlled input without a whitelist; use a whitelist of allowed origins instead.
quote: attacker can choose the value of origin

subject: Path traversal via params[:path] in Rails controllers
truth: Stop passing user-controlled params[:path] directly to File.read; validate the path using a whitelist of known good patterns or sanitize with ActiveStorage::Filename#sanitized.
quote: Accessing files using paths constructed from user-controlled data can allow an attacker to access unexpected resources.

subject: String.utf8.count / String.utf16.count / String.unicodeScalars.count used in place of String.count
truth: Stop using String.utf8.count, String.utf16.count, or String.unicodeScalars.count as a substitute for String.count; they measure different units and can cause buffer overwrites.
quote: This issue can also arise from using the values of String.utf8.count, String.utf16.count or String.unicodeScalars.count in an unsuitable place.

subject: bitwise AND bypassing control flow
truth: Stop using `&` in conditionals where the right operand is a function call with side effects; `&` always evaluates both operands, bypassing intended short-circuit control flow.
quote: `memset` will be called regardless of the value of the `len` variable
code_bad: if(len>0 & memset(buf,0,len)) return 1;
code_good: if(len>0 && memset(buf,0,len)) return 1;

subject: Client-side URL redirect from user-controlled input
truth: Stop redirecting to URLs constructed from untrusted DOM input; instead, maintain a server-side allowlist of authorized redirects and choose from that list based on user input.
quote: avoid putting user input directly into a redirect URL

subject: Complex regex without input length guard causing polynomial DoS
truth: Limit input string length to 1000 characters before matching against complex regexes like /^(\+|-)?(\d+|(\d*\.\d*))?(E|e)?([-+])?(\d+)?$/ to prevent quadratic time complexity.
quote: you can mitigate performance issues by limiting the length to 1000 characters, which will always finish in a reasonable amount of time
code_good:
if str.length > 1000
    raise ArgumentError, "Input too long"
end

is_matching = /^(\+|-)?(\d+|(\d*\.\d*))?(E|e)?([-+])?(\d+)?$/.match?(str)

subject: Ruby password hashing without computational cost
truth: Stop using general-purpose hash functions like SHA-256 for passwords; use Argon2, scrypt, bcrypt, or PBKDF2 which are computationally expensive and resist brute-force attacks.
quote: it is not suitable for password hashing since it is not computationally expensive
code_bad:
require 'openssl'

def get_password_hash(password, salt)
  OpenSSL::Digest.new('SHA256').digest(password + salt) # BAD
end
code_good:
require 'argon2'

def get_initial_hash(password)
  Argon2::Password.create(password)
end

def check_password(password, known_hash)
  Argon2::Password.verify_password(password, known_hash)
end

subject: LDAP DN construction with string concatenation
truth: Stop building LDAP DNs by concatenating user input with +; use LdapNameBuilder, UnboundID DN/RDN, or Apache Dn/Rdn constructors which automatically escape special characters.
quote: If possible build the LDAP query using framework helper methods, for example from Spring's LdapQueryBuilder and LdapNameBuilder, instead of string concatenation.
code_bad: String dn = "OU=People,O=" + organizationName;
code_good: DN safeDn = new DN(new RDN("OU", "People"), new RDN("O", organizationName));

subject: HTTP URL used with HttpsURLConnection
truth: Stop constructing URLs with the HTTP protocol when you intend to use HttpsURLConnection; use HTTPS URLs instead to ensure secure SSL connections and avoid ClassCastException.
quote: Constructing URLs with the HTTP protocol can lead to unsecured connections

subject: Runtime.exec with user-controlled script argument
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings directly as script arguments to Runtime.exec; instead validate the input against a whitelist of hard-coded script names before execution.
quote: The script to be executed by /bin/sh is controlled by the user.

subject: Tool description injection via user-controlled topic
truth: Stop concatenating user input into tool descriptions exposed to AI agents; keep tool descriptions as fixed trusted strings and pass user input via user-role messages instead.
quote: user input is used in the description of a tool exposed to the agent

subject: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Python
truth: Stop passing user input directly to requests.get() or similar HTTP request functions; validate and sanitize the input against an allowlist of permitted hosts or URL patterns.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into an HTTP request without validating the input can facilitate server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks.

subject: Sensitive data in URL query/params
truth: Stop putting sensitive information (credit card numbers, passwords, tokens) directly in URL query strings or path segments; URLs are logged, cached, and visible in cleartext.
quote: sensitive information sent in cleartext in the URL
code_bad: let body = reqwest::get(format!("https://example.com/card/{creditCardNo}")).await?.text().await?;
code_good:
let encryptedPassword = encrypt(password, encryptionKey);
let body = reqwest::get(format!("https://example.com/card/{creditCardNo}")).await?.text().await?;

subject: ReDoS-vulnerable regex with ambiguous repetition
truth: Stop using regex patterns with ambiguous repetitions (r* or r+) that can match the same input in multiple ways; rewrite the pattern to remove ambiguity or limit input length.
quote: contains a repetition of the form r* or r+ where the sub-expression r is ambiguous

subject: preprocessor defines for buffer sizes
truth: Stop hardcoding buffer sizes in multiple places; use a preprocessor define so the size is specified in one location and reused for both the declaration and the bounds check.
quote: use a preprocessor define so that the size is only specified in one place

subject: null origin in CORS
truth: Never use null as the value of Access-Control-Allow-Origin when Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is true, because an attacker can set a null origin using a sandboxed iframe.
quote: it is never safe to use null as the value of the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header when the Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header value is true

subject: External API calls with untrusted data
truth: Stop passing untrusted data directly to external APIs without treating each call as a potential sink, taint step, or uninteresting result that must be explicitly classified.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues.

subject: Manually quoting %s placeholders in SQL queries
truth: Stop manually quoting %s placeholders in SQL queries; the database connector library inserts its own quotes, so adding quotes yourself creates SQL injection vulnerabilities.
quote: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '%s'", username)
code_bad: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '%s'", username)
code_good: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = %s", username)

subject: privilege dropping order (setuid before setgid/setgroups)
truth: Always drop groups first (setgroups, setgid) before dropping user (setuid); reversing the order leaves the process with user privileges but still in the original group set.
quote: Set the new group ID, then set the target user's intended groups by dropping previous supplemental source groups and initializing target groups, and finally set the target user.

subject: Path containment check
truth: When a path should stay within a specific directory, resolve both paths to absolute form and verify the user path starts with the safe folder path plus directory separator.
quote: check that the resolved path is still contained within that directory

subject: Spring view name from user input with non-String return type
truth: Stop using user-controlled data to determine Spring view names; annotate the method with @ResponseBody or add an HttpServletResponse parameter to prevent view manipulation.
quote: returns void, so view name is taken from URI

subject: LDAPS certificate mismatch handled by disabling validation instead of fixing certificate
truth: Replace non-conforming LDAP server certificates to include a DNS name in the subjectAltName field matching the FQDN of the service, instead of disabling the endpoint check.
quote: Replace any non-conforming LDAP server certificates to include a DNS name in the subjectAltName field of the certificate that matches the FQDN of the service

subject: RMIConnectorServer instantiation without credentials filter pattern
truth: Set the key RMIConnectorServer.CREDENTIALS_FILTER_PATTERN ("jmx.remote.rmi.server.credentials.filter.pattern") to "java.lang.String;!*" when creating an RMIConnectorServer.
quote: The filter can be configured by setting the key jmx.remote.rmi.server.credentials.filter.pattern (given by the constant RMIConnectorServer.CREDENTIALS_FILTER_PATTERN).

subject: using child_process.execFile with forwarded user input
truth: Do not assume child_process.execFile is safe when the wrapper script internally forwards arguments to child_process.exec; use execFile throughout or parse with shell-quote.
quote: If another program uses child_process.execFile to invoke the above wrapper script with input from a remote user, then there may be a command-line injection vulnerability. This may be surprising, since a command-line invocation with child_process.execFile is generally considered safe.

associative: remote user input to child process

subject: Missing output filtering for prompt injection
truth: Always apply output filtering using a separate LLM call to detect if the model's response discloses system prompts or internal instructions before returning it to the user.
quote: Output filtering uses a separate LLM call to inspect the model's response and blocks it if it has leaked the system prompt or other internal instructions, complementing the input guardrail.

subject: missing overflow guard before arithmetic on tainted integers
truth: Define a guard on the arithmetic expression, so that the operation is performed only if the result can be known to be less than or equal to the maximum value for the type.
quote: Define a guard on the arithmetic expression, so that the operation is performed only if the result can be known to be less than, or equal to, the maximum value for the type

subject: Zip file extraction without path validation
truth: Stop writing zip entries directly to a file path constructed from entry.getName() without normalizing and verifying the output path stays within the destination directory.
quote: Extracting files from a malicious zip file, or similar type of archive, is at risk of directory traversal attacks if filenames from the archive are not properly validated.

subject: Java temp directory creation with mkdir() in shared temp dir
truth: Stop using File.mkdir() or Files.createDirectory() on paths under java.io.tmpdir without explicit owner-only POSIX permissions; the default permissions are world-readable.
quote: new File(System.getProperty("java.io.tmpdir"), "/child").mkdir(); // BAD: Directory has permissions `-rw-r--r--`

subject: Android certificate pinning
truth: Stop using URLConnection/openConnection without certificate pinning; implement pinning via network-security-config XML, okhttp CertificatePinner, or a custom TrustManager.
quote: By default, this network call does not use certificate pinning
code_bad: URLConnection conn = new URL("https://example.com").openConnection();
code_good:
<!-- In AndroidManifest.xml -->
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    package="com.example.app">

    <application android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/NetworkSecurityConfig">
        ...
    </application>

</manifest>

<!-- In res/xml/NetworkSecurityConfig.xml -->
<network-security-config>
    <domain-config>
        <domain>good.example.com</domain>
        <pin-set expiration="2038/1/19">
            <pin digest="SHA-256">...</pin>
        </pin-set>
    </domain-config>
</network-security-config>

subject: Spring default request mapping without explicit method
truth: Always specify an unsafe HTTP method (POST, DELETE, PUT) for state-changing endpoints; omitting the method allows safe HTTP requests which have no default CSRF protection.
quote: no HTTP request type is specified, so safe HTTP requests are allowed

subject: OGNL sandbox via system property
truth: Enable OGNL security manager by setting system property 'ognl.security.manager' or adding -Dognl.security.manager to JVM arguments before evaluating untrusted expressions.
quote: You can add a sandbox by setting a system property

subject: Log4j JNDI injection mitigation via system property
truth: Set the system property log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups to true on all client- and server-side components to disable JNDI lookups in log messages when upgrading is not possible.
quote: In previous releases (>=2.10) this behavior can be mitigated by setting system property log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups to true or by removing the JndiLookup class from the classpath (example:

subject: Android class loading without package signature check
truth: Stop loading classes from another app based solely on package name; verify the source app's identity before loading any classes/code by checking its signing certificate with PackageManager.hasSigningCertificate(String packageName, byte[] certificate, int type) (API 28+), or via getPackageInfo(packageName, PackageManager.GET_SIGNING_CERTIFICATES) and inspecting the returned PackageInfo.signingInfo. Do not use the deprecated checkSignatures(String,String)/checkSignatures(int,int) (deprecated since API 28) — they do a flat signature comparison and don't correctly account for APK Signature Scheme v3 key rotation, which can cause a legitimately re-signed app to be wrongly rejected or rotation to be mishandled into a false accept.
quote: loads classes or code from another app based solely on its package name without first checking its package signature
_audit_fix: STALE: PackageManager.checkSignatures() is deprecated (API 28+) and does not correctly handle apps that have rotated their signing certificate, which can cause the che

subject: Unsafe Reflection (Class.forName without allowlist)
truth: Stop using Class.forName with user-controlled input without an allowlist; validate the class name against a whitelist of permitted classes before reflective instantiation.
quote: Allowing users to freely choose the name of a class to instantiate could provide means to attack a vulnerable application.

subject: child_process.execSync with template literal
truth: Stop passing untrusted user input directly into child_process.execSync or exec with shell interpolation; use child_process.execFileSync with arguments as an array instead.
quote: passes untrusted user input directly to child_process.exec or similar APIs that execute shell commands
code_bad: cp.execSync(`wc -l ${file}`); // BAD
code_good: cp.execFileSync('wc', ['-l', file]); // GOOD

subject: User-controlled HTTP header names overwriting security headers
truth: Stop using user input directly as HTTP header names; sanitize to prevent overwriting security-critical headers like Access-Control-Allow-Origin or Content-Security-Policy.
quote: if the name of an HTTP header is user-controlled, an attacker may exploit this to overwrite security-critical headers such as Access-Control-Allow-Origin or Content-Security-Policy

subject: SSRF via URL concatenation with user input
truth: Stop concatenating user-controlled input directly into a request URL; instead, use user input only to select from a server-side allowlist of known-safe URLs or subdomains.
quote: user has full control of URL
code_bad: resp = requests.get("https://" + target + ".example.com/data/")
code_good:
subdomain = "europe" if target == "EU" else "world"
resp = requests.get("https://" + subdomain + ".example.com/data/")

subject: shutil.unpack_archive() tarball extraction
truth: Stop hand-validating tar member paths and stop calling shutil.unpack_archive()/tarfile extractall()/extract() without a filter. Since Python 3.12 (PEP 706), tarfile extraction accepts a `filter` argument; use `tar.extractall(path, filter='data')` (or set `TarFile.extraction_filter = tarfile.data_filter` process-wide). The 'data' filter rejects absolute paths, '..' traversal outside the destination, and absolute/outside-destination symlinks and hardlinks -- catching symlink-based traversal that a manual '..'/leading-'/' name check misses. On Python 3.12-3.13 the default is still 'fully_trusted' (with a DeprecationWarning if unset), so filter='data' must be passed explicitly; as of Python 3.14 'data' is already the default, but pass it explicitly anyway for compatibility with 3.12/3.13 and to be explicit about trust. Also pass filter='data' through shutil.unpack_archive(..., filter='data') for tar formats.
quote: Extracting files from a malicious tarball without validating that the destination file path is within the destination directory using shutil.unpack_archive() can cause files outside the destination directory to be overwritten
code_bad:
import requests
import shutil

url = "https://www.someremote.location/tarball.tar.gz"
response = requests.get(url, stream=True)

tarpath = "/tmp/tmp456/tarball.tar.gz"
with open(tarpath, "wb") as f:
      f.write(response.raw.read())

untarredpath = "/tmp/tmp123"
shutil.unpack_archive(tarpath, untarredpath)
code_good:
import requests
import tarfile

url = "https://www.someremote.location/tarball.tar.gz"
response = requests.get(url, stream=True)

tarpath = "/tmp/tmp456/tarball.tar.gz"
with open(tarpath, "wb") as f:
      f.write(response.raw.read())

untarredpath = "/tmp/tmp123"
with tarfile.open(tarpath) as tar:
	for member in tar.getmembers():
		if member.name.startswith("/") or ".." in member.name:
			raise Exception("Path traversal identified in tarball")

		tar.extract(untarredpath, member)
_audit_fix: STALE: This teaches the pre-3.12 manual-path-validation pattern; Python added a built-in extraction filter mechanism (PEP 706) in 3.12, and as of Python 3.14 the 'data

subject: resource descriptor construction
truth: Stop building resource descriptors (connection strings, file paths, URIs) via string concatenation with user input; use the dedicated builder class for the resource type.
quote: If a resource descriptor is built using string concatenation, and the components of the concatenation include user input, a user may be able to hijack the resource which is loaded.

subject: SQL query construction with JSON-marshaled data in string interpolation
truth: Stop using fmt.Sprintf with string interpolation to embed JSON-marshaled data into SQL expressions; use placeholder syntax (e.g., sq.Expr("md5(?)", versionJSON)) instead.
quote: JSON encoding does not escape single quotes in any way, so this code is vulnerable
code_bad: sq.Expr(fmt.Sprintf("md5('%s')", versionJSON))
code_good: sq.Expr("md5(?)", versionJSON)

subject: Runtime.exec with string concatenation
truth: Stop building command lines by concatenating user input into a single string passed to exec; use an array of strings instead so user input is treated only as an argument.
quote: user input might include special characters such as ampersands

subject: Exception error message exposure in HTTP responses
truth: Stop sending exception messages (ex.getMessage()) back to the remote user in HTTP error responses; log the exception server-side and send a generic error message instead.
quote: printing a exception message back to the response

subject: Bounds check for pointer arithmetic
truth: When validating a virtual method result for pointer arithmetic, check that the value is both non-negative AND less than the buffer length, not just less than the length.
quote: it is not sufficient to check that it is smaller than the length. We must also ensure that it's greater than zero, to prevent writes to locations before the buffer

subject: Spring CSRF protection disabled
truth: Stop disabling CSRF protection via csrf.disable() in Spring Security configuration; remove the .csrf(csrf -> csrf.disable()) call to keep the default enabled protection.
quote: When you use Spring, Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection is enabled by default.

subject: Log4j version detection gap in security queries
truth: Do not assume a security query can detect which version of Log4j is in use; manually verify the Log4j version in pom.xml or build files and ensure it is 2.15.0 or later.
quote: this query will not try to determine which version of Log4j is used

subject: Java regex dot matches newlines
truth: Stop using Pattern.compile(regex) without the DOTALL flag when validating access paths; use Pattern.compile(regex, Pattern.DOTALL) so the dot matches newline characters.
quote: By default, a "dot" (.) in a regular expression matches all characters except the newline characters \n and \r.
code_bad:
Pattern p = Pattern.compile(PROTECTED_PATTERN);
Matcher m = p.matcher(path);
code_good:
Pattern p = Pattern.compile(PROTECTED_PATTERN, Pattern.DOTALL);
Matcher m = p.matcher(path);

subject: ApolloServer CORS with user-controlled origin
truth: Stop passing user-controlled data directly as the CORS origin without validation; validate user-controlled origin against a whitelist of allowed origins before using it.
quote: If the origin value is user-controlled, ensure that the data is properly sanitized and validated against a whitelist of allowed origins.

subject: Flask string interpolation with user input
truth: Stop using string concatenation or f-strings with user input in Flask responses; always wrap user-controlled values with escape() or use Flask's auto-escaping templates.
quote: The first view is unsafe as first_name is not escaped, leaving the page vulnerable to cross-site scripting attacks.

subject: PAM expired credentials bypass
truth: Stop treating pam_authenticate success as sufficient for login; pam_authenticate only verifies credentials, not whether the account is expired or otherwise unauthorized.
quote: A pam_authenticate only verifies the credentials of a user. It does not check if a user has an appropriate authorization to actually login.

subject: Rust std library before main
truth: The std library only guarantees stability between the beginning and end of main; calling it from #[ctor] (before main) or #[dtor] (after main) causes undefined behavior.
quote: the std library only guarantees stability and portability between the beginning and the end of main

subject: void* pointer arithmetic as GNU extension
truth: Prefer char* to void* for byte-level pointer arithmetic, since pointer arithmetic on void* is a nonstandard GNU extension and may silently misbehave on other compilers.
quote: pointer arithmetic on void * is a nonstandard GNU extension
code_bad: return *(voidPointer + i);
code_good: return *(intPointer + i);

subject: OAuth 2.0 state parameter
truth: Stop using a constant or static string as the OAuth 2.0 state value; generate a unique, non-guessable random state per request and validate it in the redirect callback.
quote: Always include a unique, non-guessable state value

subject: ProcessBuilder environment variable names from user input
truth: Stop using user-controlled strings as environment variable names; validate both the name and value against a whitelist before adding them to the subprocess environment.
quote: an environment variable is set with a name that is derived from the user input var without validation

associative: set env var from HTTP request

subject: Path traversal sanitization via string replacement
truth: Stop trying to sanitize '../' with string replacement; it is bypassable (e.g. '.../...//' becomes '../'). Use path.resolve + fs.realpathSync + startsWith check instead.
quote: it is not sufficient to only remove "../" sequences: for example, applying this filter to "

subject: paramiko exec_command with user-controlled input
truth: Stop passing untrusted user input directly to paramiko SSHClient.exec_command(); implement proper authorization (e.g., JWT validation) before executing remote commands.
quote: the exec_command is controlled by the user and hence leads to a vulnerability
code_bad:
#!/usr/bin/env python

from flask import request, Flask
import paramiko
from paramiko import SSHClient

app = Flask(__name__)
paramiko_ssh_client = SSHClient()
paramiko_ssh_client.load_system_host_keys()
paramiko_ssh_client.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
paramiko_ssh_client.connect(hostname="127.0.0.1", port="22", username="ssh_user_name", pkey="k", timeout=11, banner_timeout=200)


@app.route('/external_exec_command_1')
def withoutAuthorization():
    user_cmd = request.args.get('command')
    stdin, stdout, stderr = paramiko_ssh_client.exec_command(user_cmd)
    return stdout

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.debug = False
    app.run()
code_good:
#!/usr/bin/env python

from flask import request, Flask
import paramiko
from paramiko import SSHClient

app = Flask(__name__)
paramiko_ssh_client = SSHClient()
paramiko_ssh_client.load_system_host_keys()
paramiko_ssh_client.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
paramiko_ssh_client.connect(hostname="127.0.0.1", port="22", username="ssh_user_name", pkey="k", timeout=11, banner_timeout=200)


@app.route('/external_exec_command_1')
def withAuthorization():
    user_cmd = request.args.get('command')
    auth_jwt = request.args.get('Auth')
    # validating jwt token first
    # .... then continue to run the command
    stdin, stdout, stderr = paramiko_ssh_client.exec_command(user_cmd)
    return stdout


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.debug = False
    app.run()

subject: Rails CSRF default behavior change in v5+
truth: Stop relying on the default protect_from_forgery behavior; in Rails 5+ the default is `with: :exception` but calling it bare downgrades to null-session which is weaker.
quote: Rails versions 5 and later automatically run protect_from_forgery with: :exception by default, but manually calling protect_from_forgery with no with argument will downgrade protection

subject: Hard-coded credentials in Ruby (Rack)
truth: Stop using plaintext password comparison for inbound authentication; use PBKDF2-HMAC with a salt and sufficient iterations per current OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet guidance (PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256: >=600,000 iterations; PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512: >=210,000-220,000; avoid SHA1 for new systems) via OpenSSL::KDF.pbkdf2_hmac — or prefer a memory-hard KDF (Argon2id, or bcrypt/scrypt) where available, since PBKDF2's lack of memory-hardness makes it the weakest of the OWASP-approved options against GPU/ASIC cracking.
quote: consider hashing passwords using standard library functions where possible. For example, OpenSSL::KDF.pbkdf2_hmac
code_bad: if password == 'myPa55word'
code_good:
hash = OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new
dk = OpenSSL::KDF.pbkdf2_hmac(
  password, salt: salt, hash: hash, iterations: 100_000, length: hash.digest_length
)
hashed_input = dk.unpack('H*').first
if hashed_password == hashed_input
_audit_fix: STALE: 100,000 iterations is well below current guidance; OWASP's Password Storage Cheat Sheet has recommended 600,000 iterations for PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 since the Dece

subject: scanf family functions without length specifier
truth: Always specify a length within the format string parameter of scanf-family functions, making it one less than the buffer size to reserve space for the NULL terminator.
quote: It is bad practice to use any of the scanf functions without including a specified length within the format parameter, as it will be vulnerable to buffer overflows.
code_bad:

            'beta.example.com',
            'www.example.com'

quote: The recommended way of writing an output file from a zip archive entry is to verify that the normalized full path of the output file starts with a prefix that matches the destination directory.

File file = new File(destinationDir, entry.getName());
FileOutputStream fos = new FileOutputStream(file); // BAD

subject: Android TextView displaying sensitive data
truth: Stop displaying sensitive information like passwords immediately in a TextView; set the view to INVISIBLE first and only reveal it on explicit user action.
quote: sensitive information in password is exposed to the TextView
code_bad:
TextView pwView = getViewById(R.id.pw_text);
pwView.setText("Your password is: " + password); // BAD: password is shown immediately

subject: Missing input validation before EL expression construction
truth: Always validate untrusted data against a strict allowlist regex before embedding it in an EL expression; otherwise arbitrary method invocation is possible.
quote: Before using untrusted data to build an EL expression, the data should be validated to ensure it is not evaluated as expression language.

subject: SSLContext default protocol in Python
truth: Stop using ssl.SSLContext(ssl_version=...) with weak protocol constants; use ssl.create_default_context() and set minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2.
quote: All versions of SSL, and TLS versions 1.0 and 1.1 are known to be vulnerable to attacks.
code_bad: context = ssl.SSLContext(ssl_version=ssl.PROTOCOL_SSLv3)
code_good:
context = ssl.create_default_context()
context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2

subject: Flask secret_key hardcoded constant
truth: Stop assigning a hardcoded string constant to app.secret_key or app.config['SECRET_KEY']; use secrets.token_hex(16) from the Python secrets module instead.
quote: Unsafe Flask SECRET_KEY Example

subject: Decompression bomb denial of service
truth: Always read decompressed data in a loop with a bounded buffer size and abort when total decompressed size exceeds a threshold to prevent denial of service.
quote: Reading decompressed file byte by byte and verifying the total current size in each loop cycle in recommended to use in any decompression library

subject: C++ exception handler memory cleanup
truth: Always check that a pointer is non-null before deleting it in a catch block, because the allocation may not have occurred before the exception was thrown.
quote: When releasing memory in a catch block, be sure that the memory was allocated and has not already been released.

subject: Dynamic code compilation from user input
truth: Stop compiling and executing source code constructed from unvalidated user input; replace with a fixed set of algorithms or a safe restricted interpreter.
quote: compiles and runs this code, returning the output. However, the user provided source code is entirely unvalidated, and therefore allows arbitrary code execution.

subject: Assembly.LoadFile path validation
truth: Do not validate assembly paths by checking file existence or extension; instead compare user input against a whitelist of known safe assembly identifiers.
quote: compare the provided input against a whitelist of known safe assemblies

subject: PreparedStatement vs Statement for SQL queries
truth: Always use PreparedStatement with parameterized queries (? placeholders) instead of Statement with string concatenation, even when inputs seem controlled.
quote: use a prepared query

subject: Rhino initStandardObjects vs initSafeStandardObjects
truth: Stop using ctx.initStandardObjects() with user-controlled scripts; use ctx.initSafeStandardObjects() or set a ClassShutter to restrict accessible classes.
quote: In the 'BAD' case, an unsafe context is initialized with initStandardObjects that allows arbitrary Java code to be executed.
code_bad:
// BAD: allow arbitrary Java and JavaScript code to be executed
Scriptable scope = ctx.initStandardObjects();
code_good:
// GOOD: enable the safe mode
Scriptable scope = ctx.initSafeStandardObjects();

subject: Certificate revocation checking disabled
truth: Do not disable certificate revocation checking by passing false to PKIXParameters.setRevocationEnabled() without providing a custom PKIXRevocationChecker.
quote: it may be disabled by passing false to the PKIXParameters.setRevocationEnabled() method

subject: Shell argument separation
truth: Stop passing dynamic file paths as part of a shell command string; pass them as separate arguments to execFileSync or spawn to avoid shell interpretation.
quote: provide the directory as an argument that does not get interpreted by a shell
code_bad:
let cmd = "rm -rf " + path.join(__dirname, "temp");
cp.execSync(cmd); // BAD

subject: DOM XSS via document.write with user input
truth: Stop writing unsanitized user input directly to the DOM via document.write; use contextual output encoding/escaping before writing user input to the page.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, a URL query parameter) to a webpage without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.

subject: Overwriting toString via prototype pollution
truth: Stop allowing user input to set object properties without sanitization; an attacker can overwrite toString and crash the application on any type coercion.
quote: replace the toString property of an object with a primitive. Whenever toString is then called on that object, either explicitly or implicitly as part of a type coercion, an exception will be raised
code_bad:
var prop = req.query.userControlled; // BAD
myObj[prop] = function() {};
code_good:
var prop = "$" + req.query.userControlled; // GOOD
myObj[prop] = function() {};

subject: lodash.merge with URL-encoded query params
truth: Stop using lodash.merge with URL-encoded query parameters; Express extended URL-encoding can inject __proto__ or constructor.prototype without JSON.parse.
quote: some web frameworks, such as Express, parse query parameters using extended URL-encoding by default

subject: URL endswith host check
truth: Stop using endswith on a URL string to validate a host; an attacker can register a subdomain like 'benign-looking-prefix-example.com' to bypass the check.
quote: the attacker must use a hostname ending in example.com, but that is easy to do

subject: ReDoS denial-of-service via exponential backtracking in Ruby regex
truth: Stop using regex patterns with polynomial or exponential worst-case time complexity on untrusted input; rewrite to remove ambiguity or limit input length.
quote: Some regular expressions take a long time to match certain input strings to the point where the time it takes to match a string of length n is proportional to nk or even 2n.

subject: Rails protect_from_forgery default null session downgrade
truth: Stop calling protect_from_forgery with no argument; always use protect_from_forgery with: :exception to raise an exception instead of nulling the session.
quote: manually calling protect_from_forgery with no with argument will still downgrade protection to provide an empty session rather than raise an exception

subject: Arithmetic on user-controlled int without overflow guard
truth: Always guard arithmetic on user-controlled ints against overflow by checking the value against INT_MAX minus the operand before performing the operation.
quote: Performing calculations on user-controlled data can result in integer overflows unless the input is validated.

subject: C++ range-based for loop with temporary container via function return
truth: Never pass a temporary container through a function that returns a reference in a range-based for loop; store the temporary in an rvalue reference first.
quote: The lifetime of the vector returned by `get_vector()` is not extended, and the behavior is undefined.

subject: Leaking system configuration via error messages
truth: Do not expose system configuration information to users in error messages; use generic messages that avoid revealing technologies, OS versions, or paths.
quote: Do not expose system configuration information to users

subject: scanf uninitialized memory read
truth: Stop reading scanf arguments without checking the return value; unassigned arguments contain indeterminate values and reading them is undefined behavior.
quote: reading one of the arguments not assigned a value is undefined behavior and may have unexpected consequences
code_bad:
scanf("%i", &i); // BAD
  return i;

subject: XPath expression with string concatenation
truth: Stop building XPath expressions via string concatenation with user input; use variable references with a custom XsltContext and XsltArgumentList instead.
quote: If an XPath expression is built using string concatenation, and the components of the concatenation include user input, a user is likely to be able to create a malicious XPath expression.

subject: Zeroed byte array as IV in GCM mode
truth: Do not initialize a byte array for an IV without filling it with random bytes; a zeroed IV is trivially predictable and breaks GCM's security guarantees.
quote: When a cipher is used in certain modes such as CBC or GCM, it requires an initialization vector (IV). Under the same secret key, IVs should be unique and ideally unpredictable.

subject: JHipster RandomUtil using RandomStringUtils without SecureRandom
truth: Stop using RandomStringUtils.randomAlphanumeric/randomNumeric without a SecureRandom; pass a SecureRandom instance to RandomStringUtils.random() instead.
quote: RandomStringUtils does not use SecureRandom
code_bad: return RandomStringUtils.randomAlphanumeric(DEF_COUNT); // BAD: RandomStringUtils does not use SecureRandom
code_good: return RandomStringUtils.random(DEF_COUNT, 0, 0, true, true, null, SECURE_RANDOM);

subject: dom4j XPath query with string concatenation
truth: Stop using document.selectSingleNode with string-concatenated XPath; use document.createXPath with SimpleVariableContext and variable references instead.
quote: The final two examples are for dom4j. They show an example of XPath injection and one method of preventing it.
code_bad: isExist = document.selectSingleNode("/users/user[@name='" + user + "' and @pass='" + pass + "']") != null;

subject: Runtime.exec command injection
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings directly to Runtime.exec. Prefer hard-coded string literals for the command/script, selecting among them based on validated user input; only sanitize/whitelist user input directly when hard-coding the command is truly impossible, since ad hoc shell-argument sanitization is notoriously hard to get fully right.
quote: passes remote user input to an arugment of a call of Runtime.exec that executes a scripting executable will allow the user to execute malicious code

subject: Runtime.exec user-controlled script
truth: Do not pass user-controlled input as the script argument to Runtime.exec; instead use hard-coded string literals to specify the command or script to run.
quote: use hard-coded string literals to specify the command or script to run

subject: Reflection without allowlist is RCE
truth: Stop passing user-controlled class names directly to Class.forName; always check the class name against a hardcoded allowlist or a single expected value.
quote: Create a list of classes that are allowed to load reflectively and strictly verify the input

subject: Denial of service via Ajv error object allocation
truth: Stop assuming allErrors: true only affects error reporting; it creates an unbounded memory allocation path that attackers can trigger with crafted input.
quote: An attacker can exploit this by sending an object that deliberately contains a huge number of errors

subject: URL logging of sensitive data
truth: Sensitive data in URLs gets logged in browser history, server logs, proxy logs, and leaks via the Referer header — stop putting secrets in query strings.
quote: Sensitive information within URLs may be logged in various locations, including the user's browser, the web server, and any forward or reverse proxy servers between the two endpoints

subject: HTML tag name case sensitivity
truth: Stop using case-sensitive regex for HTML tag names; HTML tag names can contain upper case characters and regex with IGNORECASE may still miss edge cases.
quote: HTML tag names can contain upper case characters

subject: SSRF via urllib in Python
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to urllib.request.urlopen(); validate the hostname and scheme against an allowlist before making the request.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into an HTTP request without validating the input can facilitate server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks.

subject: Role-based access control for IDOR in ASP.NET Core
truth: Use the [Authorize(Roles="...")] attribute to restrict action methods to privileged roles like administrators who are authorized to access any resource.
quote: the `Authorize` attribute is used to restrict the method to administrators, who are expected to be able to access arbitrary resources.

subject: Hard-coded password comparison in C#
truth: Stop comparing passwords against hard-coded string literals; use a hashed password from a config file verified with PasswordHasher.VerifyHashedPassword.

truth: Always validate that a search result index is >= 0 before using it to access an array; a sentinel value of -1 will cause ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
