truth: Do not rely on catching ThreadDeath to clean up after Thread.stop(); the exception can be thrown from too many locations to handle safely.
quote: it is impractical to catch all such cases

subject: Jakarta Expression Language (EL) injection via JUEL
truth: Stop concatenating untrusted user input directly into EL expressions; validate input against a strict allowlist pattern before evaluation.
quote: If an expression is built using attacker-controlled data, and then evaluated, it may allow the attacker to run arbitrary code.

subject: Spring view name from user input
truth: Stop returning user-controlled strings as view names from @Controller methods; annotate with @ResponseBody or use @RestController instead.
quote: unrestricted view name manipulation vulnerability in Spring Framework could lead to attacker-controlled arbitrary SpEL expressions being evaluated

subject: main method in Java EE web component
truth: Having a main method in the Java EE application increases the attack surface that an attacker can exploit to attack the application logic.
quote: Having a main method in the Java EE application increases the attack surface that an attacker can exploit to attack the application logic

subject: Spring URL redirect with user input
truth: Stop putting user input directly into a redirect URL; instead, validate the input against a server-side allowlist of authorized redirects.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.

subject: Express.js reflected XSS
truth: Stop writing user-controlled request parameters directly into res.send() without escaping; use escape-html or a contextual output encoder.
quote: a request parameter is incorporated without validation into the response
code_bad: res.send("Unknown user: " + req.params.id);
code_good: res.send("Unknown user: " + escape(req.params.id));

subject: HTML tag name case sensitivity
truth: Stop writing case-sensitive regex for HTML tag names; HTML tag names can contain upper case characters and regex must be case-insensitive.
quote: HTML tag names can contain upper case characters

subject: Ajv allErrors: true for debugging in production
truth: Do not enable allErrors: true in production; gate it behind an environment variable like REST_DEBUG so it only activates during debugging.
quote: A solution is to not pass in allErrors: true, which means ajv will only report the first error, not all of them
code_bad: let ajv = new Ajv({ allErrors: true });
code_good: let ajv = new Ajv({ allErrors: process.env['REST_DEBUG'] });

subject: Express.js missing X-Frame-Options header
truth: Stop sending responses without setting X-Frame-Options; call res.set('X-Frame-Options', 'DENY') before every res.send/res.json/res.render.
quote: Set the X-Frame-Options HTTP header to DENY, to instruct web browsers to block attempts to load the site in a frame

subject: Using req.cookies for authentication decisions
truth: Stop using req.cookies for authentication decisions; use req.signedCookies to ensure cookie values are server-controlled and tamper-proof.
quote: Using user-controlled data in a permissions check may allow a user to gain unauthorized access

subject: lodash.merge with untrusted input
truth: Stop using lodash.merge (or similar unsafe merge functions) with untrusted input; use lodash >= 4.17.12 or switch to a safe merge library.
quote: use of an unsafe merge or extend function to recursively copy properties from an untrusted source object

subject: Ruby backslash escaping with String#sub
truth: Stop using String#sub to backslash-escape meta-characters; it fails to escape the backslash itself and only replaces the first occurrence.
quote: backslash-escaping various meta-characters but not the backslash itself

subject: Rails CSRF null session vs exception
truth: Stop using protect_from_forgery without `with: :exception`; null-session is silent and may still allow CSRF if session parts are memoized.
quote: The default behaviour of this method is to null the session when an invalid CSRF token is provided. This may not be sufficient to avoid a CSRF vulnerability - for example if parts of the session are memoized

subject: read() return value and null termination
truth: Check the return value of read() and insert a null terminator at the last valid position before using the buffer with C string functions.
quote: In addition to checking that the read succeeds (the condition count >= 0), a null terminator is inserted before the call to strcpy.

subject: Out-of-bounds pointer dereference
truth: Ensure that pointer dereferences are properly guarded to ensure that they cannot be used to read or write past the end of the allocation.
quote: out-of-bounds read or write operation, which can cause program instability

subject: Quoting executable paths in lpCommandLine
truth: When passing NULL for lpApplicationName, always wrap the executable path in lpCommandLine with quotation marks to prevent path injection.
quote: use quotation marks around the executable path in lpCommandLine

subject: relying on umask for file permissions
truth: Do not assume the user has set a restrictive umask; always pass explicit restrictive permissions (S_IWUSR | S_IRUSR) when creating files.
quote: a program should not assume that the user will set an umask, and should still set restrictive permissions by default

subject: Input validation for code injection
truth: If dynamic code generation is unavoidable, validate user input against a whitelist to prevent arbitrary code from appearing in the input.
quote: the user input should be validated to prevent arbitrary code from appearing in the input. For example, a whitelist may be used to ensure that the input is limited to an acceptable range of values.

subject: C# password generation with System.Random
truth: Stop using System.Random for security-sensitive values like passwords; use System.Security.Cryptography.RandomNumberGenerator (e.g. RandomNumberGenerator.GetBytes(...) or RandomNumberGenerator.GetInt32(...)) instead. Note: the older RNGCryptoServiceProvider class is obsolete since .NET 6 (compiler warning SYSLIB0023) — do not recommend it as the fix.
quote: Random is not cryptographically secure, and should be avoided in security contexts
code_bad:
Random gen = new Random();
string password = "mypassword" + gen.Next();
_audit_fix: STALE: RNGCryptoServiceProvider has been marked Obsolete since .NET 6 (compiler warning SYSLIB0023); Microsoft's current guidance is to use System.Security.Cryptograph

subject: DataSet/DataTable default type limitations
truth: Do not rely on DataSet/DataTable default type limitations as a security boundary; they are not sufficient against untrusted XML payloads.
quote: DataSet and DataTable do impose default limitations on the types that are allowed to be present while deserializing XML payloads

subject: SSRF prevention via allowlist pattern
truth: Do not validate or sanitize the user-supplied URL; instead, map user input to a fixed server-side string before constructing the request.
quote: it is recommended to maintain a list of authorized request targets and choose from that list based on the user input provided

subject: Using prepared statements even without user input in Java
truth: Always use prepared statements or query parameters for supplying parameter values, whether or not parameters are traceable to user input.
quote: It is good practice to use prepared statements (in SQL) or query parameters (in the Java Persistence Query Language) for supplying parameter values to a query, whether or not any of the parameters are directly traceable to user input

subject: RabbitMQ ConnectionFactory hostname verification
truth: Always call connectionFactory.enableHostnameVerification() after connectionFactory.useSslProtocol() to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.
quote: Hostname verification for rabbitmq ConnectionFactory is not enabled
code_bad:
com.rabbitmq.client.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory = new com.rabbitmq.client.ConnectionFactory();
connectionFactory.useSslProtocol(); //BAD: Hostname verification for rabbitmq ConnectionFactory is not enabled

subject: Using SQLCipher instead of plain SQLiteDatabase
truth: Replace SQLiteDatabase.openOrCreateDatabase with net.sqlcipher.database.SQLiteDatabase.openOrCreateDatabase and pass a database password.
quote: Use SQLCipher or similar libraries to add encryption capabilities to SQLite

subject: Storing credentials in java.util.Properties that may be persisted
truth: Encrypt sensitive information before putting it into a java.util.Properties or any heap data structure that may be written to disk later.
quote: encrypt information before it is put into a heap data structure (such as Java.util.Properties) that may be written to disk later

subject: SASL authentication with ldap:// encrypts credentials
truth: When using ldap://, set SECURITY_AUTHENTICATION to a SASL mechanism like DIGEST-MD5 or GSSAPI instead of 'simple' to encrypt credentials.
quote: Use the ldaps:// protocol to send credentials through SSL or use SASL authentication.

subject: Regex injection via user input
truth: Always sanitize user input with Pattern.quote() before embedding it in a regular expression to prevent regex injection and ReDoS attacks.
quote: Constructing a regular expression with unsanitized user input is dangerous

subject: Android BroadcastReceiver accepting system intents from third-party apps
truth: A BroadcastReceiver registered for system intents must verify the intent action because third-party apps can send explicit intents to it.
quote: a BroadcastReceiver that is registered to receive system intents is still able to receive intents from a third-party application, so it should check that the intent received has the expected action

subject: Sensitive data in GET query string
truth: Stop sending sensitive information like passwords in GET query strings; use HTTP POST to send sensitive data in the request body instead.
quote: Sensitive information such as user passwords should not be transmitted within the query string of the requested URL

subject: System prompt injection via user-controlled persona
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into system-level prompts; supply user-controlled values in messages with the 'user' role instead.
quote: user input is used directly in a system-level prompt

subject: HTTP response header injection via user input
truth: Stop writing user-controlled input directly into HTTP header names or values without stripping line break characters (\n, \r) and colons.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) to an HTTP header can lead to an HTTP response-splitting vulnerability.
code_bad:
@app.route("/example_bad")
def example_bad():
    rfs_header = request.args["rfs_header"]
    response = Response()
    custom_header = "X-MyHeader-" + rfs_header
    # BAD: User input is used as part of the header name.
    response.headers[custom_header] = "HeaderValue" 
    return response
code_good:
@app.route("/example_good")
def example_bad():
    rfs_header = request.args["rfs_header"]
    response = Response()
    custom_header = "X-MyHeader-" + rfs_header.replace("\n", "").replace("\r","").replace(":","")
    # GOOD: Line break characters are removed from the input.
    response.headers[custom_header] = "HeaderValue" 
    return response

subject: SHA-256 for password hashing
truth: Stop using SHA-256 for password hashing; use Argon2 (or scrypt, bcrypt, PBKDF2) instead because SHA-256 is not computationally expensive.
quote: Although SHA-256 is a strong cryptographic hash function, it is not suitable for password hashing since it is not computationally expensive
code_good:
from argon2 import PasswordHasher

def get_initial_hash(password: str):
    ph = PasswordHasher()
    return ph.hash(password) # GOOD

def check_password(password: str, known_hash):
    ph = PasswordHasher()
    return ph.verify(known_hash, password) # GOOD

subject: Rust path traversal canonicalization check
truth: Do canonicalize the resolved path and verify it stays within the intended directory; do not rely on simple prefix checks on the raw path.
quote: ensure that the path stays within the public folder

subject: Range to NSRange conversion using incompatible lengths
truth: Stop manually converting between Range and NSRange using incompatible length values; use the appropriate initializer to convert directly.
quote: If you need to convert between Range and NSRange, do so directly using the appropriate initializer. Do not attempt to use incompatible length and offset values to accomplish conversion.

subject: process/library invocation with user-controlled path
truth: Use hard-coded string literals for the command or library path instead of passing user input directly to system, dlopen, or LoadLibrary.
quote: use hard-coded string literals for the command to run or library to load
code_bad: void* handle = dlopen(lib, RTLD_LAZY);

subject: setuid/setgid without return code check
truth: Always check the return value of setuid, setgid, setgroups, and initgroups; failing to do so silently leaves elevated privileges active.
quote: The code attempts to drop privilege in an incorrect order by erroneous dropping user privilege before groups. This has security impact if the return codes are not checked.

subject: Directly writing user input to HTTP response
truth: Stop writing raw user input to HTTP responses; always HTML-encode untrusted data using WebUtility.HtmlEncode before writing to the page.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) to a webpage, without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.

subject: ECB cipher mode
truth: Stop using ECB mode; encrypted messages are vulnerable to replay attacks because the same plaintext always produces the same ciphertext.
quote: Data is encrypted the same way every time meaning the same plaintext input will always produce the same ciphertext

subject: C# Membership.GeneratePassword bias
truth: Stop using Membership.GeneratePassword (biased output) — and do not replace it with RNGCryptoServiceProvider, which is obsolete since .NET 6 (compiler warning SYSLIB0023). Use the RandomNumberGenerator static methods instead (System.Security.Cryptography), e.g. RandomNumberGenerator.GetBytes(count) or RandomNumberGenerator.GetInt32(...), rather than instantiating RNGCryptoServiceProvider or RandomNumberGenerator.Create().
quote: In the final example, the password is generated using the Membership.GeneratePassword library method, which generates a password with a bias, therefore should be avoided.
_audit_fix: STALE: RNGCryptoServiceProvider has been obsolete in .NET since .NET 6 (compiler warning SYSLIB0023); Microsoft's current guidance is to use RandomNumberGenerator.Crea

subject: JPA named query with positional parameter in Java
truth: Use @NamedQuery with positional parameters (?1) and setParameter(position, value) instead of concatenating user input into JPQL strings.
quote: These versions are immune to injection attacks, because any special characters in the environment variable or user-supplied value are not given any special treatment.

subject: Groovy script evaluation with untrusted input
truth: Stop evaluating untrusted input with GroovyShell, Eval, or GroovyClassLoader; use a sandboxed ClassLoader that blocks dangerous classes.
quote: avoid using untrusted input in a Groovy evaluation

subject: Android Dex loading storage location
truth: Do not use /sdcard or any external/shared storage for Dex files; always use getCacheDir() or getFilesDir() which are private to the app.
quote: Loading a file from private storage instead of a world-writable one can prevent this issue
code_bad: File file = new File("/sdcard/updater.apk");
code_good: File file = new File(getCacheDir() + "/updater.apk");

subject: Client IP from X-Forwarded-For header
truth: Stop trusting the first entry of X-Forwarded-For; use the last entry if behind a reverse proxy, or use request.getRemoteAddr() directly.
quote: Attackers can forge the value of these identifiers to bypass a ban-list

subject: Storing API methods in global scope
truth: Do not store API methods in the global scope; put them in an API object or Map and use hasOwnProperty to whitelist allowed method names.
quote: Instead of storing the API methods in the global scope, put them in an API object or Map.

subject: JavaScript object property assignment from user input
truth: Stop using user-controlled strings directly as object property keys; prepend a marker character like '$' to prevent prototype pollution.
quote: prepend untrusted input with a marker character such as $, before using it in properties accesses
code_bad:
var prop = req.query.userControlled; // BAD
myObj[prop] = function() {};
code_good:
var prop = "$" + req.query.userControlled; // GOOD
myObj[prop] = function() {};

subject: Nokogiri XML parsing with NOENT option enables XXE
truth: Stop using Nokogiri::XML::ParseOptions::NOENT or config.noent when parsing untrusted XML; omit the option or use config.nonoent instead.
quote: the parser is invoked with the noent option set
code_bad: doc = Nokogiri::XML.parse(xmlSrc, nil, nil, Nokogiri::XML::ParseOptions::NOENT) # BAD
code_good: doc = Nokogiri::XML.parse(xmlSrc) # GOOD

subject: fgets into fixed-size buffer without validation
truth: Stop using fgets to read untrusted data into a fixed buffer without validating or sanitizing the input before passing it to other APIs.
quote: input is read from fgets and then ultimately used in a call to the fputs external API

subject: code before first case label
truth: Stop placing executable statements between the switch opening brace and the first case label; that code is dead and will never execute.
quote: i1=c1*2; // BAD: the code will not be executed

subject: Buffer access with incorrect length value
truth: Stop hard-coding a literal size for a buffer argument; use sizeof(buf) to derive the correct length from the actual buffer declaration.
quote: You need to limit the value of the length argument
code_bad:
char buf[256];
X509_NAME_oneline(X509_get_subject_name(peer),buf,1024); // BAD
code_good:
char buf[256];
X509_NAME_oneline(X509_get_subject_name(peer),buf,sizeof(buf)); // GOOD

subject: ValidationType.DTD on user-provided XML
truth: Do not set ValidationType to DTD for user-provided XML; set it to Schema so validation uses a known schema, not the document's own DTD.
quote: If it is set to DTD, the document is only validated against the DTD specified in the user-provided document itself - which could be specified as anything by a malicious user.

subject: Assembly.LoadFile with user input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to Assembly.LoadFile; validate the path against a whitelist of known safe assemblies first.
quote: Load assembly based on user input

subject: Go stored XSS with io.WriteString
truth: Stop writing stored values directly to HTTP responses; import "html" and call html.EscapeString() on every externally-controlled value.
quote: Sanitizing the file names prevents the vulnerability
code_bad: io.WriteString(w, file.Name()+"\n")
code_good: io.WriteString(w, html.EscapeString(file.Name())+"\n")

subject: Backslash handling in URL redirects
truth: Replace all backslashes with forward slashes before parsing a redirect URL, because some browsers treat backslashes as forward slashes.
quote: some browsers treat backslashes in URLs as forward slashes. To account for this, we replace all backslashes with forward slashes before parsing the URL and checking its hostname.

subject: strconv.Atoi to int32 conversion
truth: Stop using strconv.Atoi when you need a specific bit size; use strconv.ParseInt or strconv.ParseUint with the correct bit size instead.
quote: If a string is parsed into an int using strconv.Atoi, and subsequently that int is converted into another integer type of a smaller size, the result can produce unexpected values.

subject: Serializable objects containing cleartext sensitive data
truth: Always encrypt sensitive information inside serializable or marshallable objects, unless you are certain they will never be serialized.
quote: Objects that are serializable or marshallable should also always contain encrypted information unless you are certain that they are not ever going to be serialized

subject: JHipster password reset token generation using predictable PRNG
truth: Stop using RandomStringUtils for security-sensitive tokens; initialize a static SecureRandom and pass it to RandomStringUtils.random().
quote: attackers can request a password reset token and use this to predict the value of future reset tokens

subject: No input validation on reflective class loading
truth: Stop reflective class loading without validation; add an allowlist check before Class.forName to prevent arbitrary class instantiation.
quote: strictly verify the input to ensure that users can only instantiate classes or execute methods that ought to be allowed.

subject: Hardcoded hex-decoded import path
truth: Stop decoding hardcoded hex string literals and using them as import paths; use a normal require() with a plain string literal instead.
quote: hexadecimal constant decoded and interpreted as import path

subject: Open redirect validation via URL parsing
truth: Do not assume a relative path is safe; parse the URL against a known base origin and verify the host stays the same before redirecting.
quote: check that we don't redirect to a different host

subject: loading external scripts over HTTP
truth: Always use HTTPS URLs when loading external scripts and consider adding subresource integrity (SRI) checks via the integrity attribute.
quote: Including a resource from an untrusted source or using an untrusted channel may allow an attacker to include arbitrary code in the response.
code_bad: <script src="http://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.6.0.slim.min.js" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
code_good: <script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.6.0.slim.min.js" integrity="sha256-u7e5khyithlIdTpu22PHhENmPcRdFiHRjhAuHcs05RI=" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>

subject: SSRF via user-controlled hostname in outgoing HTTP request
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into the hostname of an outgoing HTTP request URL; pick the hostname from an allow-list instead.
quote: Avoid user input in the hostname of the URL. Pick the hostname from an allow-list instead of constructing it directly from user input.

subject: os.path.join does not prevent path traversal
truth: Stop assuming os.path.join(base, user_input) is safe; a user input like '/etc/passwd' or '../../etc/passwd' escapes the base directory.
quote: Note that a user could also use an absolute path here, since the result of os.path.join("/server/static/images/", "/etc/passwd") is "/etc/passwd".
code_bad: data = open(os.path.join(base_path, filename), 'rb').read()
code_good:
fullpath = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base_path, filename))
if not fullpath.startswith(base_path):
    raise Exception("not allowed")
data = open(fullpath, 'rb').read()

subject: Django environ default constant secret
truth: Stop using a constant string as the default value in django-environ's Env() for SECRET_KEY; the default fallback is a hardcoded secret.
quote: following is not safe if there is default value in Env(..)

subject: LDAP authentication with empty password
truth: Stop using an empty string or None as the password when binding to an LDAP server; use a non-empty password to prevent anonymous binds.
quote: anonymous binds causes an empty or None-set password to result in a successful authentication
code_bad: ldap_connection.bind('cn=root', "")
code_good: ldap_connection.bind('cn=root', "SecurePa$$!")

subject: Client IP from X-Forwarded-For header
truth: Stop trusting the first entry of X-Forwarded-For for security checks; use the last entry instead, as reverse proxies append to the end.
quote: the less-trustworthy first one is used
code_good:
client_ip = request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for')
client_ip = client_ip.split(',')[client_ip.split(',').length - 1]
if not client_ip == '127.0.0.1':
    raise Exception('ip illegal')

subject: CORS with allow_credentials=True and wildcard origins
truth: Do not set allow_origins to '*' when allow_credentials=True; any external site can then make authenticated requests on behalf of users.
quote: can allow any Peer B to send requests to the misconfigured Peer A on behalf of the user
code_bad:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

app = FastAPI()

origins = [
    "*"
]

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)


@app.get("/")
async def main():
    return {"message": "Hello World"}
code_good:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

app = FastAPI()

origins = [
    "http://localhost.tiangolo.com",
    "https://localhost.tiangolo.com",
    "http://localhost",
    "http://localhost:8080",
]

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)


@app.get("/")
async def main():
    return {"message": "Hello World"}

subject: File content loaded with .html_safe in ERB
truth: Stop using .html_safe when loading file content into script tags; ensure the file is trusted and cannot be modified by untrusted users.
quote: Care should taken in these cases to ensure both that the loaded files are trusted, and that the file cannot be modified by untrusted users.

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL in reqwest::get
truth: Stop passing user input directly to reqwest::get; validate the input against an allowlist of known-safe URLs before making the request.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into an HTTP request without validating the input can facilitate server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks

subject: Passing remote data to WKWebView JavaScript
truth: Do not inject remote data into JavaScript source strings; pass remote data objects via the arguments dictionary of callAsyncJavaScript.
quote: send it to the web view as data using an API such as WKWebView.callAsyncJavaScript with the arguments dictionary to pass remote data objects
code_bad: _ = try await webview.evaluateJavaScript("console.log(" + remoteData + ")") // BAD
code_good:
_ = try await webview.callAsyncJavaScript(
    "console.log(data)",
    arguments: ["data": remoteData], // GOOD
    contentWorld: .page
)

subject: atoi without overflow validation before arithmetic
truth: Validate user-controlled integer input against INT_MAX minus the operation magnitude before performing arithmetic to prevent overflow.
quote: Always guard against overflow in arithmetic operations on user-controlled data

subject: C++ iterator to expired temporary container
truth: Never create an iterator to a temporary container when the iterator is expected to be used after the container's lifetime has expired.
quote: Using an iterator owned by a container after the lifetime of the container has expired can lead to undefined behavior

subject: Persistent cookies with sensitive data
truth: Stop setting sensitive information in persistent cookies; use session cookies (no expiration) or short-lived cookies (≤5 min) instead.
quote: Do not put sensitive information in persistent cookies.

subject: DataSet/DataTable default type limitations
truth: Do not assume default type limitations in DataSet/DataTable make XML deserialization safe; they are still unsafe with untrusted input.
quote: DataSet and DataTable do impose default limitations on the types that are allowed to be present while deserializing XML payloads, DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: Logging sensitive data in Go
truth: Stop logging sensitive information like passwords in cleartext; omit them entirely or log only non-sensitive fields like the username.
quote: Sensitive information that is logged unencrypted is accessible to an attacker who gains access to the logs.
code_bad: log.Printf("Registering new user %s with password %s.\n", user, pw)
code_good: log.Printf("Registering new user %s.\n", user)

subject: Android SharedPreferences storing sensitive data
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cleartext via SharedPreferences. Do not use androidx.security.crypto.EncryptedSharedPreferences for new code — the whole androidx.security:security-crypto library (including EncryptedSharedPreferences and EncryptedFile) was deprecated by Google in security-crypto 1.1.0-alpha07 (April 9, 2025) due to main-thread performance issues and keyset-corruption bugs on some OEM devices. Use Jetpack DataStore with Google Tink's StreamingAead (encrypting the whole file, keys held in Android Keystore) for encrypted key-value storage instead.
quote: sensitive information should not be saved in cleartext
_audit_fix: STALE: EncryptedSharedPreferences (and the whole androidx.security:security-crypto library) was deprecated by Google starting at security-crypto 1.1.0-alpha07 (April 2

subject: Trust boundary violation in Java servlets
truth: Always validate user-supplied input before writing it to the session; never write request parameters directly into session attributes.
quote: The input is written to the session without being sanitized.

subject: Android file leak via unprotected intent data path
truth: Stop loading file content from an external activity result without checking that the returned URI path does not start with /data/data.
quote: the intent could refer to paths which are accessible to the receiver application, but are intended to be application-private

subject: Client-side request forgery via open redirect chaining
truth: Avoid user input in the hostname of the URL; pick the hostname from an allow-list instead of constructing it directly from user input.
quote: Avoid user input in the hostname of the URL. Pick the hostname from an allow-list instead of constructing it directly from user input.

subject: Unvalidated user input in server-side HTTP request
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings directly into server-side HTTP requests; validate with an allowlist (numeric/alphanumeric) first.
quote: avoid putting user input directly into a network request. If using user input is necessary, then is mandatory to validate them.

subject: Paramiko SSH client missing host key policy
truth: Do not set the missing host key policy to AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy; use RejectPolicy to throw an exception on unknown host keys.
quote: Do not accept unknown host keys. In particular, do not set the default missing host key policy for the Paramiko library to either AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy.
code_bad: client.set_missing_host_key_policy(AutoAddPolicy)
code_good: client.set_missing_host_key_policy(RejectPolicy)

subject: Ruby shell command injection via exported functions
truth: Exported functions that build shell commands with string interpolation expose callers to command injection; pass arguments separately.
quote: a malicious user to provide the input http://example.org; cat /etc/passwd in order to execute the command cat /etc/passwd
code_bad: system("wget #{path}")
code_good: system("wget", path)

subject: URL redirect host validation
truth: Stop redirecting to any user-supplied URL; parse the URI and verify the host is either absent (relative) or matches a known good host.
quote: check that the target URL does not redirect to a different host by checking that the URL is either relative or on a known good host
code_bad:
class HelloController < ActionController::Base
  def hello
    redirect_to params[:url]
  end
end
code_good:
require 'uri'

class HelloController < ActionController::Base
  KNOWN_HOST = "example.org"

  def hello
    begin
      target_url = URI.parse(params[:url])

      # Redirect if the URL is either relative or on a known good host
      if !target_url.host || target_url.host == KNOWN_HOST
        redirect_to target_url.to_s
      else
        redirect_to "/error.html" # Redirect to error page if the host is not known
      end
    rescue URI::InvalidURIError
      # Handle the exception, for example, by redirecting to a safe page
      redirect_to "/error.html"
    end
  end
end

subject: Rails mass assignment with User.new(params)
truth:
Fix both code fields, not just code_good, since code_bad is equally broken (identical to code_good). Set: code_bad = "User.new(params[:user]).save!" (or "User.new(params.require(:user).permit!).save!" to show the permit! blanket-allow variant CodeQL rb/mass-assignment also flags) — i.e. raw/unfiltered params reaching mass assignment, allowing attacker-controlled keys like is_admin. Set: code_good = "def user_params
  params.require(:user).permit(:name, :email)
end

User.new(user_params).save!" — Strong Parameters with an explicit attribute allowlist, matching the reviewer's suggestion. Keep 'truth' text as-is; it is already correct.
quote: mass assignment operation from user supplied parameters
_audit_fix: WRONG: code_bad and code_good are byte-for-byte identical ("User.new(user_params).save!" in both fields), so the fact shows no actual fix and teaches nothing distingui

subject: Rust SHA-2 family for passwords
truth: Stop using SHA-2 (SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512) for password hashing; all SHA-2 variants are weak against offline brute forcing.
quote: All of MD5, SHA-1, SHA-2 and SHA-3 are weak against offline brute forcing, so they are not suitable for hashing passwords

subject: Format string construction via string interpolation
truth: Stop building format strings dynamically with string interpolation or concatenation; use constant format strings with %@ placeholders.
quote: Use a constant string literal for the format string to prevent the possibility of data flow from an untrusted source
code_bad: print(String(format: "User input: " + inputString)) // vulnerable
code_good: print(String(format: "User input: %@", inputString)) // fixed

subject: strcat as taint step
truth: For C/C++ code generation, the real strcat landmine is buffer overflow, not a taint-modeling gap: strcat performs no bounds checking on the destination buffer, so appending an oversized/attacker-influenced source string can overflow it (CodeQL flags this as CWE-119/120, severity 9.8). Do not use raw strcat; prefer strncat with an explicit remaining-capacity check, or snprintf/strlcat-style length-checked concatenation, and always verify the destination buffer has room for the source plus a null terminator before concatenating.
quote: this external API is not currently modeled as a taint step in the taint tracking library
_audit_fix: WRONG: strcat is already modeled as a built-in taint-propagating function in CodeQL's C++ standard library (StrcatFunction/TaintFunction); the quoted 'not currently mo

subject: SSL certificate verification result
truth: Always call SSL_get_verify_result after SSL_get_peer_certificate and check that the result equals X509_V_OK before trusting the peer.
quote: it is unsafe to use that information without checking if the certificate is valid
code_bad: X509 *cert = SSL_get_peer_certificate(ssl); // BAD (SSL_get_verify_result is never called)

subject: temporary std::string c_str() pointer
truth: Stop storing c_str() from a temporary std::string; declare the string as a named local variable so its lifetime outlives the pointer.
quote: the memory returned by `c_str()` is freed when the temporary string is destroyed
code_bad:
const char* p = std::string("hello").c_str();
c_api(p);

subject: CSharpCodeProvider with user input
truth: Do not use CSharpCodeProvider to compile source code that contains any unvalidated user input; this enables arbitrary code execution.
quote: If the application dynamically compiles and runs source code constructed from user input, a malicious user may be able to run arbitrary code.

subject: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) in ASP.NET WebForms
truth: Always verify that the current user is authorized to access the resource identified by the ID parameter before performing any action.
quote: If the methods do not check that the current user is authorized to access the specified resource, an attacker can access a resource by guessing or otherwise determining the linked ID parameter.

subject: time-bomb backdoor using file modification timestamp offset
truth: Stop using file modification timestamp offsets as conditional triggers; this pattern matches known backdoor implants like Solorigate.
quote: an offset to a last file modification time is used to conditionally execute a particular block of code

associative: scheduled trigger based on file time

subject: External API usage with untrusted data
truth: Stop calling external APIs with unsanitized untrusted data; validate and sanitize all inputs before passing them to external methods.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues

subject: Environment variable as command
truth: Stop using environment variables or other user-controlled strings as command names; validate against a whitelist of allowed commands.
quote: takes a shell script that can be changed maliciously by a user, and passes it straight to Runtime.exec without examining it first

subject: JWT parsing with jjwt's parse() method
truth: On current JJWT (0.12+), Jwts.parserBuilder() no longer exists — Jwts.parser() itself now returns a JwtParserBuilder. Never call parse()/parseClaimsJwt()/parseClaimsJws() without first configuring a verification key (or accept tokens with alg=none), since that skips signature verification. Instead build the parser with a required verification key — Jwts.parser().verifyWith(key).build() (verifyWith, replacing the deprecated setSigningKey()) — then call parseSignedClaims(token) (parseClaimsJws() still works but is deprecated in favor of parseSignedClaims()) to actually verify the signature before trusting any claims.
quote: Unfortunately the parse method accepts a JWT whose signature is empty although a signing key has been set for the parser.
code_bad:
Jwts.parserBuilder()
                .setSigningKey("someBase64EncodedKey").build()
                .parse(token); // BAD: Does not verify the signature
code_good:
Jwts.parserBuilder()
                .setSigningKey("someBase64EncodedKey").build()
                .parseClaimsJws(token) // GOOD: Verify the signature
                .getBody();
_audit_fix: STALE: JJWT 0.12+ removed the Jwts.parserBuilder() method entirely — Jwts.parser() itself now returns a JwtParserBuilder (must call .build() before parsing), and .setS

subject: Piggybacking on prior authentication across channels
truth: Do not assume a user authenticated on one channel is the same user connecting on a separate channel; authenticate on the new channel.
quote: an attacker may be able to connect to the alternate channel before the legitimate user does

subject: XQuery injection via string concatenation
truth: Stop building XQuery expressions by concatenating user input into the query string; use bindString to parameterize the query instead.
quote: The software uses external input to dynamically construct an XQuery expression

subject: Path traversal via path.join with unsanitized query parameter
truth: Stop passing unsanitized req.query data to path.join; validate and sanitize the file path input before constructing filesystem paths.
quote: the result of the path.join will be used as a file path, leading to a path traversal vulnerability

subject: URL encoding is not a defense against SSRF
truth: Stop relying on URL encoding to prevent SSRF; it does not block path traversal or internal endpoint access behind proxies like NGINX.
quote: URL encoding is not a solution in certain scenarios, such as, an architecture build over NGINX proxies.

subject: Flask Jinja2 XSS
truth: Rendering user input through Jinja2 templates without autoescape silently produces unescaped HTML, enabling stored and reflected XSS.
quote: The first view is unsafe as first_name is not escaped, leaving the page vulnerable to cross-site scripting attacks

subject: Regex catastrophic backtracking in Ruby < 3.2
truth: Do not rely on Ruby's regex engine being safe; Ruby < 3.2 has no caching and ambiguous repetitions cause exponential time complexity.
quote: Ruby 3.2 and later have implemented a caching mechanism that completely eliminates the worst-case time complexity for the regular expressions flagged by this query

subject: Unhandled error in reqwest response
truth: Never build the request URL for reqwest::get (or other reqwest request builders) directly from unvalidated user input — doing so enables Server-Side Request Forgery (CWE-918, CodeQL rust/request-forgery), letting an attacker redirect the server's outbound request to an internal or unintended host. Validate the host/URL against an allowlist of trusted, known-safe destinations before issuing the request, rather than passing user-supplied URLs through unchecked.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into an HTTP request without validating the input
_audit_fix: WRONG: The actual CodeQL rs/request-forgery rule (CWE-918 SSRF) is about validating user-controlled URLs against an allowlist before making an HTTP request with reqwes

subject: Password hashing without computational hardness
truth: Use a password hashing algorithm that is slow and/or memory intensive (Argon2, scrypt, bcrypt, PBKDF2) to resist brute force attacks.
quote: Password hashing algorithms should be slow and/or memory intensive to compute, to make brute force attacks more difficult

subject: Decrypting sensitive data only at the point of use
truth: Decrypt sensitive information only at the point where it is necessary for it to be used in cleartext, minimizing exposure in memory.
quote: decrypt sensitive information only at the point where it is necessary for it to be used in cleartext

subject: OpenSSL Heartbleed missing bounds check
truth: Stop trusting attacker-supplied size values in network protocols without validating they are within the bounds of the source buffer.
quote: payload is supplied as part of the remote request, and there is no code that checks the size of it

subject: Pointer arithmetic with sizeof scaling
truth: Stop manually scaling pointer offsets by sizeof(T); pointer arithmetic is already automatically scaled by the size of the data type.
quote: Pointer arithmetic in C and C++ is automatically scaled according to the size of the data type
code_bad: return *(intPointer + (i * sizeof(int)));
code_good: return *(intPointer + i);

subject: reverse DNS lookup for authentication
truth: Stop using reverse DNS lookup results as a security decision; use numeric IP addresses or a cryptographic session mechanism instead.
quote: An attacker can return an incorrect reverse DNS entry for the requesting IP and thus gain the same access as a legitimate user

subject: temporary file creation with hardcoded predictable path
truth: Stop using hardcoded predictable paths like /tmp/name.tmp for temporary files; use mkstemp with a template ending in XXXXXX instead.
quote: Working with a file, without checking its existence and its rights, as well as working with names that can be predicted, may not be safe.
code_bad: fp = fopen("/tmp/name.tmp","w"); // BAD

subject: ASP.NET customErrors mode
truth: Set customErrors mode to 'On' or 'RemoteOnly' in Web.config; do not leave it 'Off' which leaks stack traces via default error pages.
quote: Set the customErrors to On to prevent the default error page from being displayed
code_bad:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <customErrors mode="Off">
      ...
    </customErrors>
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <customErrors mode="On">
      ...
    </customErrors>
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: Token validation defaults
truth: Do not disable token validation by setting properties to false; the secure default is true, so remove the assignment or set to true.
quote: remove the assignment to false because the default value is true.

subject: DataSet/DataTable XML deserialization
truth: Stop using DataSet or DataTable to deserialize untrusted XML input; use a safer serializer or validate input before deserialization.
quote: DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: dangerous native function calls (P/Invoke)
truth: Stop calling NtWriteVirtualMemory via P/Invoke to write to remote processes; this is a common code injection and backdoor technique.
quote: finds native calls to external functions that are often used in creating backdoors

subject: Stack trace details in user-facing error messages
truth: Do not include server-side file names, SQL code, or other internal details from stack traces in error messages sent to remote users.
quote: The error message at the top of a stack trace can include information such as server-side file names and SQL code

subject: Android file encryption with EncryptedFile
truth: androidx.security:security-crypto (and EncryptedFile within it) was deprecated at 1.1.0-alpha07 (April 2025); MasterKeys was already deprecated earlier (since 1.1.0-alpha01) in favor of MasterKey.Builder. For new code, prefer Android Keystore-backed encryption directly or Google Tink instead of Jetpack Security Crypto. If EncryptedFile/security-crypto must still be used (e.g. legacy code), build the master key with MasterKey.Builder(context).setKeyScheme(MasterKey.KeyScheme.AES256_GCM) rather than the deprecated MasterKeys.AES256_GCM_SPEC helper — but treat the whole EncryptedFile/EncryptedSharedPreferences path as unsupported going forward, since Google will not patch further bugs in it.
quote: the whole file is encrypted with androidx.security.crypto.EncryptedFile
_audit_fix: STALE: androidx.security.crypto's MasterKeys class is deprecated in favor of MasterKey.Builder, and the whole androidx.security:security-crypto library itself was depr

subject: JHipster RandomUtil using insecure PRNG for authentication tokens
truth: Stop using RandomStringUtils.randomAlphanumeric/randomNumeric for authentication tokens; use SecureRandom-backed randomness instead.
quote: CVSS v3.0 Base Score of 9.8/10
code_bad: return RandomStringUtils.randomAlphanumeric(DEF_COUNT); // BAD: RandomStringUtils does not use SecureRandom

subject: TOCTOU race condition on synchronized objects
truth: Synchronized methods on the resource class do not prevent state changes between separate method calls from the caller's perspective.
quote: it does not prevent a state change in between separate method invocations

subject: Java regex library choice for linear-time matching
truth: Stop relying on Java's default backtracking regex engine for untrusted input; use RE2J or another linear-time regex library instead.
quote: an alternate regex library that guarantees linear time execution, such as Google's RE2J, may be used

subject: NashornScriptEngine with -scripting flag
truth: Stop using NashornScriptEngineFactory.getScriptEngine() with the -scripting flag on user input; it enables arbitrary code execution.
quote: Execute externally controlled input in Nashorn Script Engine

subject: Library functions constructing HTML via innerHTML
truth: Stop using innerHTML with string concatenation for user-controlled input; use safe DOM APIs like innerText or createElement instead.
quote: does not escape unsafe HTML

subject: Regex-based HTML tag expansion breaking attribute boundaries
truth: Never use regex to transform HTML tags; the regex can match inside attribute values, breaking attribute boundaries and enabling XSS.
quote: the function is not safe

subject: Accidental exposure of package.json _where field
truth: Stop exposing the entire node_modules folder; an attacker can read the _where field from package.json revealing absolute file paths.
quote: the _where field from a package.json file, which gives access to the absolute path of the file
code_bad: app.use('/node_modules', express.static(path.resolve(__dirname, '../node_modules')));
code_good:
app.use("jquery", express.static('./node_modules/jquery/dist'));
app.use("bootstrap", express.static('./node_modules/bootstrap/dist'));

subject: Hex-decoding function for obfuscated strings
truth: Stop writing functions that hex-decode hardcoded string literals to use as code or import paths; use plain string literals directly.
quote: takes a hard-coded string literal, hex-decodes it and then uses it as an import path

subject: SSRF via path traversal in request URL pathname
truth: Restrict user input in the pathname of the URL so that path traversal ('../') cannot redirect the request to an unintended endpoint.
quote: Restrict the input so that path traversal ("../") cannot be used to redirect the request to an unintended endpoint.

subject: Dynamic code evaluation via Worker with user-controlled URL
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to the Worker constructor; validate and restrict the URL to a safe allowlist of scripts.
quote: Directly evaluating user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) as code without properly sanitizing the input first allows an attacker arbitrary code execution.

subject: Jinja2 select_autoescape
truth: Use select_autoescape(['html', 'xml']) instead of manually setting autoescape to handle escaping correctly for different file types.
quote: Jinja2 provides the function select_autoescape to make sure that the correct auto-escaping is chosen
code_bad: unsafe_env = Environment(loader=loader)
code_good: safe2_env = Environment(loader=loader, autoescape=select_autoescape())

subject: Werkzeug debugger arbitrary code execution
truth: Stop exposing the Werkzeug debugger in production; the interactive debugger allows arbitrary code execution via the /crash endpoint.
quote: By visiting /crash, it is possible to gain access to the debugger, and run arbitrary code through the interactive debugger

subject: Storing sensitive data in cookies
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cookies in plain text; encrypt it using the cryptography module or store a lookup key instead.
quote: stores user credentials (in this case, their password) in a cookie in plain text

subject: ssl.wrap_socket deprecated
truth: Stop using ssl.wrap_socket (deprecated since Python 3.7); use ssl.create_default_context or ssl.SSLContext with minimum_version set.
quote: Note that ssl.wrap_socket has been deprecated in Python 3.7.
code_bad: ssl.wrap_socket(socket.socket())
code_good:
context = ssl.create_default_context()
context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2

subject: XSLT injection via user-controlled stylesheet
truth: Stop passing untrusted user input directly as an XSL stylesheet to etree.XML; validate and sanitize all XSL input before processing.
quote: Processing an unvalidated XSL stylesheet can allow an attacker to change the structure and contents of the resultant XML, include arbitrary files from the file system, or execute arbitrary code.

subject: Flask config file default fallback constant
truth: Stop using a constant string as the default fallback in os.getenv() for SECRET_KEY; check and raise if the default is still present.
quote: # if we are loading SECRET_KEY from config files then

subject: LDAP3 connection with password=None
truth: Stop passing password=None when creating an LDAP3 Connection; always provide a non-empty password to avoid anonymous authentication.
quote: anonymous binds causes an empty or None-set password to result in a successful authentication
code_bad: conn = Connection(srv, user='user_dn', password=None)
code_good: conn = Connection(srv, user='user_dn', password="SecurePa$$!")

subject: Parsed host substring check
truth: Stop checking if the parsed host contains an allowed domain as a substring; use an explicit whitelist of complete hostnames instead.
quote: This is still not a sufficient check as the following URLs bypass it: http://evil-example.com http://example.com.evil-example.net
code_bad:
class AppController < ApplicationController
    def index
        url = params[:url]
        host = URI(url).host
        # BAD: the host of `url` may be controlled by an attacker
        if host.include?("example.com")
            redirect_to url
        end
    end
end
code_good:
class AppController < ApplicationController
    def index
        url = params[:url]
        host = URI(url).host
        # GOOD: the host of `url` can not be controlled by an attacker
        allowedHosts = [
            'example.com',
            'beta.example.com',
            'www.example.com'
        ]
        if allowedHosts.include?(host)
            redirect_to url
        end
    end
end

subject: URI.open in Ruby
truth: Stop using URI.open with user input; use Net::HTTP.get(URI.parse(...)) or an HTTP client instead to prevent shell command injection.
quote: The same vulnerability applies to
code_bad: URI.open(web_page) # BAD - calls `Kernel.open` internally
code_good: Net::HTTP.get(URI.parse(web_page))

subject: unsigned subtraction compared to zero with signed cast fix
truth: Stop comparing unsigned subtraction to zero; cast the operands to a signed type large enough to hold the result before subtracting.
quote: consider casting the result of the subtraction to a signed type

subject: std::string::c_str() lifetime
truth: Store the concatenated std::string in a named variable before calling c_str() on it, so the underlying buffer outlives the pointer.
quote: the underlying std::string object that represents the concatenated string is destroyed as soon as the call to c_str returns

subject: Cast before pointer arithmetic on char*
truth: When a char* points to an array of a different type, cast to the correct type before using pointer arithmetic, e.g. (double*)p + k.
quote: Cast to the correct type before using pointer arithmetic

subject: Logging private information
truth: Stop logging private information (like addresses) to log files; ensure private information is only stored in secure data locations.
quote: Private information that is stored in an external location may be more vulnerable

subject: Command whitelist in Go
truth: Instead of interpreting stored input directly as command names, examine the input and then choose among hard-coded string literals.
quote: examine the input and then choose among hard-coded string literals

subject: missing upper bound check on user-controlled allocation size
truth: Stop passing user-controlled integers directly to make() without first enforcing a maximum size limit to prevent memory exhaustion.
quote: Implement a maximum allowed value for size allocations with the built-in make function to prevent excessively large allocations.

subject: partial path traversal via getCanonicalPath()
truth: Stop using getCanonicalPath() with startsWith() to check directory containment; use Path#normalize() and Path#startsWith() instead.
quote: getCanonicalPath() is not slash-terminated

subject: Non-slash-terminated canonical path prefix check
truth: getCanonicalPath() returns a non-slash-terminated string, so startsWith() allows sibling directory access instead of only children.
quote: getCanonicalPath() returns a non-slash-terminated path string

subject: Android WebView JavaScript default
truth: Do not assume JavaScript is needed; it is disabled by default and should remain disabled unless loading only trusted HTTPS content.
quote: JavaScript execution is disabled by default. You can explicitly disable it by calling setJavaScriptEnabled(false) on the settings of the WebView.
code_bad:
WebSettings settings = webview.getSettings();
settings.setJavaScriptEnabled(true); // BAD: webview has JavaScript enabled
code_good:
WebSettings settings = webview.getSettings();
settings.setJavaScriptEnabled(false); // GOOD: webview has JavaScript disabled

subject: SpEL expression evaluation with user-controlled input
truth: Stop calling expression.getValue() without a restricted context; always pass a SimpleEvaluationContext when user input is involved.
quote: If user input must be included in the expression, it should be then evaluated in a limited context that doesn't allow arbitrary method invocation.

subject: Android content provider unintended access via Intent reflection
truth: Reflecting a user-provided Intent via setResult can grant arbitrary access to your content providers even if they are not exported.
quote: the application may be unintentionally giving arbitrary access to its content providers, even if they are not exported

subject: RSA cipher without OAEP padding
truth: Use OAEP padding (e.g., RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding) instead of NoPadding or PKCS1Padding when instantiating an RSA Cipher.
quote: Using an outdated padding scheme such as PKCS1, or no padding at all, can weaken the encryption by making it vulnerable to a padding oracle attack.
code_bad: Cipher rsa = Cipher.getInstance("RSA/ECB/NoPadding");
code_good: Cipher rsa = Cipher.getInstance("RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding");

subject: Zip entry path used directly as filesystem path
truth: Do not use the entry path from a zip archive directly as a filesystem path; always validate that it does not contain '..' elements.
quote: If these file paths are used to create a filesystem path, then a file operation may happen in an unexpected location.

subject: Temporary file naming with timestamps
truth: Stop using Date.now() or Math.random() to name temp files; use tmp.fileSync() which guarantees a non-existing, non-accessible file.
quote: there is no guarantee that the file does not already exist
code_bad:
const file = path.join(os.tmpdir(), "test-" + (new Date()).getTime() + ".txt");
fs.writeFileSync(file, "content");
code_good:
const file = tmp.fileSync().name;
fs.writeFileSync(file, "content");

subject: Passing arguments as array to subprocess.run
truth: Stop building shell commands with string concatenation; pass arguments as an array to subprocess.run to avoid shell interpretation.
quote: provide the dynamic arguments to the shell as an array to APIs such as subprocess.run to avoid interpretation by the shell

subject: Flask redirect with user input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to redirect(); validate the target against an allowlist or check that it has no netloc.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.

subject: Rails CSRF protection disabled via skip_before_action
truth: Stop disabling CSRF token verification with skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token; remove the call to re-enable protection.
quote: CSRF protection is disabled by skipping token verification

subject: String(format:) with concatenated user input
truth: Stop concatenating user input into the format string; pass user-controlled strings as format arguments using %@ specifiers instead.
quote: Passing untrusted format strings to functions that use printf style formatting can lead to buffer overflows and data representation problems
code_bad: print(String(format: "User input: " + inputString)) // vulnerable
code_good: print(String(format: "User input: %@", inputString)) // fixed

subject: Hardcoded encryption keys in Swift
truth: Stop using hardcoded arrays or constant strings as encryption keys; use SecRandomCopyBytes to generate random key material instead.
quote: Hardcoded keys should not be used for creating encryption ciphers
code_bad:
let key: Array<UInt8> = [0x2a, 0x3a, 0x80, 0x05]
let keyString = "this is a constant string"
let ivString = getRandomIV()
_ = try AES(key: key, blockMode: CBC(AES.randomIV(AES.blockSize)), padding: padding)
_ = try AES(key: keyString, iv: ivString)
_ = try Blowfish(key: key, blockMode: CBC(Blowfish.randomIV(Blowfish.blockSize)), padding: padding)
_ = try Blowfish(key: keyString, iv: ivString)

subject: strcat with untrusted data
truth: Stop assuming strcat is a sink; model strcat as a taint step because tainted data flows from the 1st argument to the 0th argument.
quote: tainted data would flow from the 1st argument to the 0th argument of the call

subject: unbounded allocation from user input
truth: Reject the request before proceeding further if the input value fails the range guard, so subsequent calculations cannot overflow.
quote: If the input value fails this guard then reject the request before proceeding further.
code_bad: char **root_node = (char **) malloc(factor * sizeof(char *));

subject: Expired SSL certificate acceptance
truth: Stop treating expired certificates as valid; an expired certificate should not be accepted as it is more likely to be compromised.
quote: An expired certificate should not be accepted as it is more likely to be compromised than a valid certificate

subject: Conditionally uninitialized variable from init function
truth: Always check the return status code of an initialization function before reading the local variable it was supposed to initialize.
quote: the code does not check the return value from the function call to initDeviceConfig

subject: vector erase inside loop with iterator invalidation
truth: Stop using the old iterator after vector::erase; assign the return value of erase to the iterator and do not increment separately.
quote: the iterator is invalidated after the call to `erase`

subject: Storing sensitive data in cookies in cleartext
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cookies as cleartext; use MachineKey.Protect to encrypt the value before setting the cookie.
quote: Setting a cookie value with cleartext sensitive data
code_bad: ctx.Response.Cookies["AccountName"].Value = accountName;
code_good: ctx.Response.Cookies["AccountName"].Value = Protect(accountName, "Account name");

subject: Runtime.exec with user-controlled input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings directly to Runtime.exec; validate and restrict input to hard-coded command literals instead.
quote: passes user input directly to Runtime.exec

subject: MVEL expression from socket input
truth: Stop reading untrusted data from a socket and passing it directly to MVEL.eval(); the expression can invoke arbitrary JVM methods.
quote: the user-provided expression is directly evaluated

subject: Android component with intent filter
truth: Always explicitly set android:exported on every component that has an intent filter; omitting it implicitly exports the component.
quote: If the android:exported attribute is omitted from the component when an intent filter is included, then the component will be implicitly exported.
code_bad:
<activity>
            android:name=".Activity">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
code_good:
<activity>
            android:name=".Activity">
            android:exported="false"
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>

subject: Jython PythonInterpreter.exec() with user input
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input to PythonInterpreter.exec(); use a sandboxed interpreter or avoid dynamic evaluation entirely.
quote: BAD: allow execution of arbitrary Python code

subject: Tomcat JSESSIONID HttpOnly flag
truth: Stop setting useHttpOnly=false in web.xml; Tomcat 7+ sets HttpOnly on session cookies by default and you should not override this.
quote: you should not override this behavior

subject: Tomcat session cookie HttpOnly default
truth: Stop disabling HttpOnly on session cookies; Tomcat 7+ enables it automatically and overriding to false exposes session IDs to XSS.
quote: automatically sets an HttpOnly flag on all session cookies

subject: Java RMI deserialization filter
truth: Set an ObjectInputFilter via UnicastRemoteObject.exportObject(Remote, int, ObjectInputFilter) to restrict deserialization classes.
quote: Set a filter for incoming serialized data by wrapping remote objects using either UnicastRemoteObject.exportObject(Remote, int, ObjectInputFilter) or UnicastRemoteObject.exportObject(Remote, int, RMIClientSocketFactory, RMIServerSocketFactory, ObjectInputFilter) methods.
code_bad: registry.bind("unsafe", new RemoteObjectImpl());

subject: Spring redirect: string concatenation with 'redirect:' prefix
truth: Stop using string concatenation with 'redirect:' prefix and user input; validate the redirect target against an allowlist instead.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.

subject: Unmodeled external API as taint step hides downstream vulnerabilities
truth: Model external APIs like path.join as taint steps so downstream sinks are detected; otherwise vulnerabilities are silently missed.
quote: this external API is not currently modeled as a taint step in the taint tracking library

subject: shell commands requiring pipes or I/O redirection
truth: When a shell is needed (pipes, redirections), escape user input with shellQuote.quote() before embedding it in the command string.
quote: use shell-quote to escape any special characters in the input before embedding it in the command

subject: undocumented unsafe code construction in library APIs
truth: Document library functions that construct code from unsanitized inputs as potentially unsafe, or avoid constructing code entirely.
quote: If the function is not documented as being potentially unsafe, then a client may incorrectly use inputs containing unsafe code fragments

subject: Backdoor via file exfiltration
truth: Stop embedding file contents into HTTP request headers without validation; this pattern is used by real backdoors in npm packages.
quote: backdoor code that was identified in two popular npm packages

subject: Client-side request forgery via path traversal in fetch URL
truth: Restrict user input in the URL pathname to prevent path traversal attacks; do not concatenate unsanitized user input into the URL.
quote: the format of the message ID is not checked, and an attacker can abuse this to alter the endpoint targeted by the request

associative: build api url with user input

subject: Logging sensitive information
truth: Be aware that external processes often store stdout and stderr streams, causing logged sensitive information to be stored as well.
quote: external processes often store the standard out and standard error streams of the application, causing logged sensitive information to be stored as well

subject: Django url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme
truth: Use Django's url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme() instead of manual urlparse checks; it handles backslashes correctly out of the box.
quote: url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme handles backslashes correctly, so no additional processing is required.

subject: Hard-coded credentials in Ruby (Rack)
truth: Stop storing plaintext passwords in source code; load hashed passwords and salts from a separate config file (e.g., YAML) instead.
quote: Remove hard-coded credentials, such as user names, passwords and certificates, from source code, placing them in configuration files
code_bad: if password == 'myPa55word'
code_good:
config_file = YAML.load_file('config.yml')
hashed_password = config_file['hashed_password']
salt = [config_file['salt']].pack('H*')

subject: External API calls with untrusted data
truth: Stop passing unsanitized untrusted data to external APIs without first modeling whether the API is a sink, a taint step, or safe.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues

subject: backslash-a in Go regexp string literals
truth: Use raw string literals (backticks) for Go regexp patterns to avoid confusing \a (bell character) with \A (start-of-text anchor).
quote: regexp.Compile("\a") matches the bell character, whereas regexp.Compile("\\A") matches the start of text

subject: unmodeled external API calls with untrusted data
truth: Do not ignore external API calls receiving untrusted data; model unknown sinks and taint steps to uncover hidden vulnerabilities.
quote: external APIs that use untrusted data

subject: Newline stripping before logging in Go
truth: Stop assuming user input is safe for logs; strip \n and \r characters using strings.ReplaceAll before logging plain-text entries.
quote: line breaks should be removed from user input, using strings.Replace or similar

subject: JWT library default behavior
truth: Do not assume a JWT library verifies signatures by default; use ParseWithClaims which explicitly performs signature verification.
quote: Always verify the signature by using the appropriate methods provided by the JWT library, or use a library that verifies it by default.
code_bad: jwt.NewParser().ParseUnverified(token, &User{})

subject: XPath parameterization in Go
truth: Pre-compile the XPath query and use variable references ($varname) resolved via goxpath.Opts instead of concatenating user input.
quote: pre-compile the query and use variable references to include the user input

subject: MVEL expression evaluation
truth: Stop evaluating untrusted user input directly with MVEL.eval(); validate the expression against an allow-list or deny-list first.
quote: the user-provided expression is directly evaluated
code_bad: MVEL.eval(expression);
code_good:
validateExpression(expression);
MVEL.eval(expression);

subject: Android KeyStore biometric key generation
truth: Stop setting setUserAuthenticationRequired(false); set it to true to require biometric authentication before the key can be used.
quote: setUserAuthenticationRequired should be set to true; otherwise, the key can be used without user authentication.

subject: WebView debugging enabled in production
truth: Guard WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled(true) behind a debug-build check; never enable unconditionally in production builds.
quote: debugging is always enabled
code_bad: WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled(true);

subject: ReDoS via complex number regex without input length limit
truth: When a regex cannot be easily rewritten to avoid catastrophic backtracking, limit input length (e.g., 1000 chars) to prevent DoS.
quote: It is not immediately obvious how to rewrite this regular expression to avoid the problem.

subject: ResultReceiver from Intent
truth: Do not send sensitive data to a ResultReceiver obtained from an untrusted Intent; validate the receiver or avoid sending secrets.
quote: Do not send sensitive data to an untrusted ResultReceiver

subject: JShell eval in loop with analyzeCompletion
truth: Stop calling JShell.eval() on results from analyzeCompletion() when the input is attacker-controlled; it executes arbitrary code.
quote: avoid using untrusted input in a JShell expression

subject: Java RMI process-wide deserialization filter
truth: Set a process-wide deserialization filter via ObjectInputFilter.Config.setSerialFilter() or the jdk.serialFilter system property.
quote: It is also possible to set a process-wide deserialization filter. The filter can be set by with ObjectInputFilter.Config.setSerialFilter(ObjectInputFilter) method, or by setting system or security property jdk.serialFilter.

subject: Spring unsafe exporter CVE-2016-1000027
truth: Do not assume Spring's HttpInvokerServiceExporter is safe; CVE-2016-1000027 is a design limitation that cannot be fixed outright.
quote: CVE-2016-1000027 has been assigned to this issue in the Spring Framework. It is regarded as a design limitation, and can be mitigated but not fixed outright.

subject: Multi-character regex sanitization for script tags
truth: Stop matching the entire script tag with a multi-character regex; instead match single characters '<' and '>' to remove all tags.
quote: A fix for this issue is to rewrite the regular expression to match single characters ("<" and ">") instead of the entire unsafe text.
code_bad: str.replace(/<script\b[^<]*(?:(?!<\/script>)<[^<]*)*<\/script>/g, "");

subject: Empty password in configuration file
truth: Stop using empty strings as passwords in configuration files; choose a strong password and encrypt it if stored in a config file.
quote: The use of an empty string as a password in a configuration file is not secure.

subject: Jinja2 SandboxedEnvironment for untrusted templates
truth: If constructing a template from user input is absolutely necessary, use SandboxedEnvironment.from_string() instead of Template().
quote: A sandboxed environment is used to construct the template
code_bad:
from django.urls import path
from django.http import HttpResponse
from jinja2 import Template, escape


def a(request):
    template = request.GET['template']

    # BAD: Template is constructed from user input. 
    t = Template(template)

    name = request.GET['name']
    html = t.render(name=escape(name))
    return HttpResponse(html)


urlpatterns = [
    path('a', a),
]
code_good:
from django.urls import path
from django.http import HttpResponse
from jinja2 import escape
from jinja2.sandbox import SandboxedEnvironment


def a(request):
    env = SandboxedEnvironment()
    template = request.GET['template']

    # GOOD: A sandboxed environment is used to construct the template. 
    t = env.from_string(template)

    name = request.GET['name']
    html = t.render(name=escape(name))
    return HttpResponse(html)


urlpatterns = [
    path('a', a),
]

subject: Django CSRF protection commented out during testing
truth: Stop leaving CSRF middleware commented out after testing; uncomment 'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware' before deploying.
quote: The protecting middleware was probably commented out during a testing phase, when server-side token generation was not set up. Simply commenting it back in will enable CSRF protection.

subject: Full SSRF via subdomain injection in Python requests
truth: Stop concatenating user input into the hostname portion of a URL; use server-side mapping from input to a fixed subdomain string.
quote: user has full control of URL
code_bad: resp = requests.get("https://" + target + ".example.com/data/")
code_good:
subdomain = "europe" if target == "EU" else "world"
resp = requests.get("https://" + subdomain + ".example.com/data/")

subject: Rust ctor/dtor functions calling std library
truth: Do not call any part of the std library from a #[ctor] or #[dtor] function; use a non-std alternative like libc_println! instead.
quote: Calling functions and methods in the Rust std library from a #[ctor] or #[dtor] function is not safe

subject: Allowlist validation for shell commands
truth: Do not sanitize or escape shell metacharacters; instead validate user input against a hard-coded allowlist of permitted commands.
quote: use hard-coded string literals to specify the command to run

subject: memcpy buffer size mismatch
truth: When using memcpy, limit the copy size to the smaller of source and destination buffer lengths to prevent overread or overwrite.
quote: reduce the size of the memcpy to the smaller of the source and destination buffers, min(256, strlen(message) + 1)

subject: SQL connection string construction
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into SQL connection strings; use SqlConnectionStringBuilder to safely include user input.
quote: Direct use of user input in a connection string passed to SqlConnection
code_bad:
string connectionString = "server=(local);user id=" + userName + ";password= pass;";
SqlConnection sqlConnectionBad = new SqlConnection(connectionString);

subject: allocation size calculation without overflow guard
truth: Always guard against integer overflow in allocation size calculations by validating input data length before computing the size.
quote: Always guard against overflow in arithmetic operations involving potentially large numbers

subject: SSH host key callback returning nil
truth: Stop writing HostKeyCallback functions that unconditionally return nil; always validate the host key against a known allow list.
quote: a semantically similar callback, should not be used in production code

subject: Android APK installation from external storage
truth: Stop installing APKs using an Intent with data from external/uncontrolled storage; use PackageInstaller or FileProvider instead.
quote: If the file used in the Intent is from a location that is not controlled by the application (for example, an SD card that is universally writable), this can result in the unintended installation of untrusted applications.

subject: Android cleartext password storage
truth: Stop storing passwords or sensitive data in cleartext on the Android filesystem; encrypt individual fields or use EncryptedFile.
quote: sensitive information should not be saved in cleartext

subject: Android LocalBroadcastManager for in-app broadcasts
truth: Do not send sensitive intra-app data via global broadcasts (sendBroadcast/registerReceiver on the system BroadcastManager) — other apps can register receivers and intercept it. androidx LocalBroadcastManager, once the standard fix, is itself deprecated since 1.1.0 (Jan 2022, no further releases) — Google's current guidance is to use LiveData, Kotlin Flow/StateFlow (e.g. via a shared ViewModel), or another in-process observable/callback mechanism instead, which is both lifecycle-aware and inherently confined to the process (no Intent/broadcast surface at all).
quote: a LocalBroadcastManager may be used
_audit_fix: STALE: androidx LocalBroadcastManager has been deprecated by Google since Android 11 (2021) with no further releases planned, so recommending it as the fix directs the

subject: Timing side-channel via String.equals() on secrets
truth: Stop using String.equals() for secret comparison; use MessageDigest.isEqual() which runs in constant time regardless of content.
quote: the comparison time should not depend on the content of the input
code_bad: return Key.equals(request.getHeader("X-Auth-Token"));
code_good: return MessageDigest.isEqual(Key.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8), token.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));

subject: jQuery HTML string auto-detection
truth: Stop relying on jQuery's auto-detection of HTML strings; jQuery() silently parses strings starting with < as HTML, creating XSS.
quote: the call to jQuery interprets sourceSelector as HTML if it is a string that starts with <
code_bad: var source = jQuery(options.sourceSelector),
code_good: var source = jQuery.find(options.sourceSelector),

subject: Unsigned cookie trust assumption
truth: Stop treating unsigned cookies as server-controlled data; all cookie values are user-controlled unless cryptographically signed.
quote: all of these sources of data are user-controlled, so a malicious user can easily bypass such checks

subject: prototype pollution via __proto__
truth: An attacker can inject properties into Object.prototype by passing {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} to an unsafe merge function.
quote: if src is the object {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}, it will inject the property isAdmin: true in Object.prototype.

subject: Unescaped dash in regex character class
truth: Always escape the dash character in a regex character class, or place it at the start/end, to prevent unintended range creation.
quote: failing to escape the dash character in a regular expression

subject: CORS origin validation using startswith
truth: Stop using string.startswith() to validate CORS origins; compare the origin string with exact equality (==) against a whitelist.
quote: This check can be bypassed easily by origin like domain.com.baddomain.com

subject: Interpreting hardcoded data as code
truth: Stop dynamically interpreting hardcoded string literals as code or import paths; always use literal paths and avoid obfuscation.
